urn:contentItem:6JH7-9DR3-S2YK-H1B5-00000-00CROWLEY MARITIME - RECENT NEWS FROM CMA CGM LOGISTICS SA AND INDUSTRY PEERS2026-05-05T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:36:34ZLexisNexisSTOCKCROWLEY MARITIME - RECENT NEWS FROM CMA CGM LOGISTICS SA AND INDUSTRY PEERS

DASHBOARD: CROWLEY MARITIME

PermID4295904276Websitehttp://www.crowley.com/IndustryMaritime & Shipping Logistics

ACTIVITIES:

Crowley Maritime Corporation, founded in 1892, is a family-owned logistics and maritime services company headquartered in Jacksonville, Florida. Employing over 5,300 people, it operates in shipping, energy, and supply chain management, generating over $2 billion annually. Crowley provides ocean transport, LNG distribution, and logistics for government and commercial clients. In 2024, it expanded renewable energy logistics, supporting offshore wind projects. Its culture emphasizes safety, innovation, and sustainability, with robust employee training. Competing with Maersk, Crowley stands out for its U.S.-focused operations and government contracts. Recent initiatives include decarbonization efforts and digital tracking systems, aligning with green logistics trends.

+ CROWLEY MARITIME [3 News That Matters]

+ CROWLEY MARITIME ADVANCES ALASKA GROWTH WITH PORT MCKENZIE FUEL TERMINAL FEASIBILITY

Crowley Maritime has announced a plan to conduct a feasibility study for a potential fuel terminal at Port McKenzie, signaling a broader push to expand its Alaska footprint. The permit was granted on January 7, 2026, as rising regional activity underscores demand for improved logistics and transportation capabilities.Officials say the terminal would provide a reliable fuel-supply chain for local marine and land operations, supporting growing commercial activity in the Matanuska-Susitna Borough and the broader Alaska economy.Crowley's strategy centers on leveraging Port McKenzie's location to enhance logistics and attract other tenants. If developed, the project could streamline Crowley's operations and help catalyze additional investment in the area.With the permit in place, Crowley moves forward with feasibility assessments and engagement with local stakeholders, marking a potential milestone for the company and for Port McKenzie as they explore future growth opportunities.

+ CROWLEY MARITIME ANNOUNCES DUAL-DIVISION REALIGNMENT TO ENHANCE OPERATIONAL AGILITY

Crowley Maritime announced on January 6, 2026 the formation of two new divisions—Shipping and Logistics led by James C. Fowler, and Energy led by Kollin Fencil—as part of a strategic consolidation to improve execution and accelerate growth across its shipping, logistics and energy activities.The Shipping and Logistics Division combines Crowley's logistics, land transportation and global ship management to serve commercial and government customers with ocean shipping, inland trucking, multimodal transportation and warehousing.Fowler said the reorganized structure will enable cohesive management of the entire transportation value chain, driving growth through safe and efficient supply-chain solutions.The Energy Division consolidates Crowley Fuels in Alaska, LNG services and advanced energy solutions, enabling greater innovation and scale in both traditional and emerging energy markets.Fencil noted the realignment will help Crowley meet evolving customer demands for traditional fuels and advanced energy solutions, and the leaders have delivered improvements in financial performance and operational efficiency since joining the company in 2023. Chief Operating Officer Megan Davidson said the realignment will speed decision-making and improve the company's ability to respond to market dynamics, reinforcing Crowley's status as a leader in maritime, energy and logistics. Crowley is privately held and U.S.-owned, with a footprint spanning 36 nations and territories and more than 130 years in business.

+ FAIRWATER HOLDINGS EMERGES AS NEW JONES ACT SERVICE PROVIDER FROM CROWLEY-SEACOR ALLIANCE

Crowley Maritime and SEACOR Holdings have created Fairwater Holdings, a new joint venture intended to integrate their liquid energy and chemical transportation assets and to serve as an independent U.S. Jones Act service provider.The venture will combine the strengths of Crowley and SEACOR to offer transportation solutions across the petroleum and chemical trades, plus third-party ship management.Fairwater will manage a fleet of 20 ocean-going articulated tug-barges and 11 tankers, largely under long-term charters, and will provide crewing and technical management for an additional 21 vessels owned by third parties.Daniel Thorogood, currently CEO of Seabulk, will take the helm as CEO of Fairwater once the deal closes.Crowley's Tom Crowley described Seabulk as an ideal partner with a proven track record, adding the venture supports Crowley's growth strategy and strengthens its position as the largest American-flag petroleum and chemical tank vessel fleet, with capacity exceeding 12 million barrels.The companies expect to begin operations after the customary closing conditions and regulatory approvals, with Fairwater slated to commence in the first quarter of 2024.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ CMA CGM LOGISTICS [3 News That Matters]

+ CMACGM EXPANDS INDIAN FLAG REGISTRY WITH FIFTH VESSEL

MUMBAI: CMA CGM Group announced the reflagging of its fifth container vessel in India, saying the move will streamline services on key routes. The company noted the reflagging occurred on the 24th of April 2026, reinforcing its long-term commitment to India.The reflagging changes the Vila do Conde's registration to the Indian flag and involves a 2,592 TEU capacity vessel.This effort fits a broader government push to grow an Indian-flag fleet, with Prime Minister Modi emphasizing self-sufficiency in shipping.CMA CGM has already reflagged five vessels since last year and plans a sixth by the end of May.Earlier this year, the company ordered six dual-fuel vessels from Cochin Shipyard for about $350 million and announced a planned $300 million investment in Maharashtra's logistics infrastructure. Executives are also exploring inland waterways and proposed using Gujarat International Finance Tec-City, or GIFT City, as a vessel-registration hub to strengthen maritime security and competitiveness.

+ CMACGM'S GEMALINK EXPANSION SET TO DOUBLE VIETNAM PORT CAPACITY BY 2027

CMA CGM Group has announced Phase 2 of the Gemalink terminal expansion in Cai Mep port, southern Vietnam, with the work beginning on 20th April 2026. The move reinforces CMA CGM's commitment to expanding port capacity in Southeast Asia and supporting robust, sustainable supply chains in the region. When finished, capacity will nearly double from 1.7 million TEUs to around 3 million TEUs, with full operation expected by 2027. The plan adds 450 meters of quay in two segments, expands the container yard from 32 to 44 hectares, and introduces five additional Ship-to-Shore cranes, bringing the total to thirteen. Gemalink has quickly established itself as one of the region's most efficient terminals since its 2021 opening, currently operating at full capacity and handling 1.7 million TEUs annually. Its location along key trade lanes makes it a strategic hub within CMACGM's network in Vung Tau. Christine Cabau Woehrel, CMA CGM Group Executive Vice President, said the expansion reflects a long-term partnership with Gemadept and aligns with Vietnam's strategy to become a leading maritime logistics hub in Southeast Asia by 2050.

+ CMA CGM ENHANCES TRANSATLANTIC OFFERING WITH DIRECT CORK CALLS TO NEW YORK AND SAVANNAH

CMA CGM Group has announced enhancements to its Transatlantic PAD service, adding Cork as a direct weekly link to New York and Savannah, effective 28th April 2026.The changes include deployment of an additional vessel and optimized port rotations to improve schedule reliability and overall predictability for supply chains relying on these routes.The PAD service now features direct calls at Dunkerque and Zeebrugge, a distinguishing feature not offered by other services, and connects with CMA CGM's Northern Europe network including Le Havre and Rotterdam to facilitate feedering to Baltic and Scandinavian markets.In the United States, the service benefits from CMA CGM Port Liberty terminals, providing priority berthing to help relieve port congestion and bolster service reliability.The PAD service is capable of handling pharmaceuticals, beverages, and general cargo, appealing to time sensitive and high value shipments and offering a competitive alternative to air freight. The Cork addition strengthens Ireland's access to major U.S. markets, complementing CMA CGM's two-decade presence in Ireland across three ports and up to six weekly sailings, linking the country to more than 420 ports worldwide and supporting key sectors such as pharmaceuticals, agri-food, and manufacturing.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

Fri Apr 24, 2026: Duke Energy Secures 20-Year License for Robinson Nuclear Plant

Duke Energy Corporation has received a significant boost with the U.S. Nuclear Regulatory Commission's (NRC) decision to grant a 20-year extension for the operating license of its Robinson Nuclear Plant. This renewal allows the facility to continue producing energy until the year 2050, a crucial development for the utility giant. The announcement was made on April 23rd, 2026, and signifies Duke Energy's commitment to providing stable and sustainable energy to the region.

The Robinson Nuclear Plant, located in Hartsville, South Carolina, plays a vital role in Duke Energy's operations, supplying electricity to approximately 570,000 households. This license extension not only secures the plant's operational future but also aligns with Duke Energy's broader strategy of maintaining a diverse energy portfolio. The company is focused on integrating nuclear, natural gas, and renewable energy sources to meet the growing energy demands of its 8 million customers across various states, including the Carolinas, Indiana, Florida, Ohio, and Kentucky.

Duke Energy operates within the regulated utilities sector, boasting a market capitalization of approximately $99.64 billion. The company is segmented into electric utilities and infrastructure, gas utilities and infrastructure, and commercial renewables. Currently, the company's P/E ratio stands at 20.26, which is close to its five-year median, indicating that the stock may be fairly valued in the current market context. Investors are encouraged to consider this valuation alongside other financial metrics when evaluating the stock's potential.

The GF Score(TradeMark) for Duke Energy is 77 out of 100, reflecting strong performance across key metrics that can lead to higher long-term returns. The company's profitability rank is notably strong at 7 out of 10, underscoring its operational efficiency. However, concerns regarding financial strength persist, with a rating of just 3 out of 10, suggesting potential issues related to debt levels and overall financial health. Investors should carefully weigh these factors when considering Duke Energy as part of their investment portfolio.

In recent months, insider trading activity has raised eyebrows, with insiders selling $5.6 million worth of shares and no recorded insider buying. This trend may lead to questions about insider confidence in the company's future performance. Overall, Duke Energy presents a mixed outlook for investors. While the recent license renewal for the Robinson Nuclear Plant strengthens its operational foundation, its financial challenges could impact long-term growth. Investors are encouraged to explore further insights about Duke Energy and its current market position.

Recent News 2 of 3, Source: HourlyBusinessNews, 271 words

Wed Apr 22, 2026: Duke Energy Honored with Arbor Day Foundation’s Tree Line USA Award for 20th Year

Duke Energy Corporation has received national recognition for its tree care practices for the 20th consecutive year. The Arbor Day Foundation named Duke Energy Florida a 'Tree Line USA' utility, citing the company's rigorous tree pruning standards, comprehensive employee training, and public education efforts aimed at reducing power outages and safeguarding electrical infrastructure.

On 22nd April 2026, the Arbor Day Foundation lauded Duke Energy for meeting industry standards for tree care, supporting tree planting initiatives, and engaging in annual arbor education programs. The designation reflects Duke Energy's commitment to sustainable practices, as well as its proactive approach to maintaining the health of urban trees in proximity to electrical lines.

One of Duke Energy's ongoing initiatives includes an annual free tree giveaway for customers through the Arbor Day Foundation's Energy-Saving Trees program. In 2026, the company distributed 1,000 trees to Florida customers, bringing the total number of free trees given away statewide to over 16,000 since 2017. These trees are shipped directly to customers with detailed planting instructions designed to encourage proper placement away from power lines.

Company officials noted that proper tree placement is essential for conserving energy and minimizing the risk of outages during storm and hurricane seasons. Duke Energy also conducts year-round vegetation maintenance near power lines and ramps up these efforts ahead of hurricane season, ensuring a reliable power supply for its customers.

Additionally, Duke Energy reminds customers not to trim trees near power lines themselves and encourages them to contact Sunshine 811 before digging near underground utilities. More information about responsible tree planting near electrical equipment can be found through Duke Energy's Right Tree Right Place program.

Recent News 3 of 3, Source: HourlyBusinessNews, 395 words

Wed Apr 22, 2026: Duke Energy Foundation Launches America250 Grants for South Carolina Green Spaces

GREENVILLE, S.C.– To strengthen communities across South Carolina as the nation approaches its 250th anniversary, Duke Energy Foundation is awarding $275,000 to nonprofit organizations through a first round of funding as part of its America250 initiative. This funding, announced on 22nd April 2026, celebrates Earth Day and aims to enhance local parks, tree planting efforts, and trail maintenance projects throughout the Palmetto State.

The grants, which form part of a total investment exceeding $550,000 in South Carolina, are aimed at revitalizing parks and shared green spaces, supporting tree planting, and litter prevention initiatives. "As we look back on our country's 250-year anniversary, it's an opportunity to reflect on our nation's history while working to support the next generation," said Tim Pearson, Duke Energy South Carolina president. "We are proud to partner with local organizations who can help care for the Palmetto State now, so future generations can enjoy its beauty."

Duke Energy Foundation has awarded a total of 11 grants in this first round of funding. Notable recipients include the South Carolina Wildlife Federation, Foothills Trail Conservancy, and the Upstate Greenways & Trails Alliance. Each organization is expected to utilize the funding to enhance local environments and foster community engagement in conservation efforts. For instance, Upstate Greenways & Trails Alliance plans to create a new trail that highlights the scenic beauty of Lake Keowee while educating visitors about the Revolutionary War history in the area.

The positive responses from grant recipients highlight the importance of such initiatives. Matt Flege, Executive Director of Upstate Greenways & Trails Alliance, expressed gratitude for the support, stating, "The America250 grant will allow us to create a new trail showcasing the beauty of Lake Keowee to South Carolina residents and visitors alike." Similarly, Susan H. Wild, Chair of Sumter Green, emphasized how the investment strengthens their commitment to beautifying the community, ensuring that efforts made today will benefit future generations.

These grants mark the first awards under Duke Energy Foundation's America250 initiative, which represents a commitment of over $1 million towards community-driven projects across the company's six-state service territories. Additional grant recipients will be announced later this spring, including initiatives focused on veterans' workforce pathways and enhancing access to history and civics education. Duke Energy Foundation continues to provide nearly $30 million annually in philanthropic support to the communities it serves, reflecting its dedication to environmental stewardship and community development.

SECTION 2 TODAY'S BULLISH SIGNALS

2.1 Uptrend

Today's Volatility:

The 4% discount to 12-month high of $134.49 against the 20% premium to 12-month low of $107.42 suggests the stock continues to push for new highs.

Today its volatility (highest price minus lowest price/lowest price) of 1.6% equalled the average daily volatility, up from 1.2584091808468567% on Wednesday. Price/Moving Average Price of 1.05:

- The Price/MAP 200 for Duke Energy Corporation is 1.05. Being higher than 1 is a bullish indicator. The stock is trading above both its MAPs and the 50-day MAP of $133.03 is higher than the 200-day MAP of $122.48, a second bullish indicator. The 200-day MAP has increased to $122.48, a third bullish indicator.

Past Quarter:

- In the last three months the stock has hit a new 52-week high twelve times, pointing to a significant uptrend.

2.2 Other Bullish Signals

- Return on Equity of 9.0% versus sector average of 5.8%.

MCap/Total Assets:

- Tobin's Q Ratio, defined as MCap divided by Total Assets, is 0.5. Compared with the rest of the market the stock is undervalued.

- Net profit margin has averaged 14.4% in the last 3 years. This is better than the sector average of 11.8% and suggests a high margin of safety.

- As per the Du Pont analysis, Return on Equity of 9.0% is better than sector average of 5.8%. This is computed as net profit margin of 15.2% times asset turnover [sales/assets] of 0.16 times leverage factor [total assets/shareholders' equity] of 3.6.

3.2 Created Market Value [CMV] past 17 yrs of $79.3 billion

- Market Capitalization has increased by $81.3 billion from $18.6 billion to $99.9 billion in the last 17 years. This increase comprises cumulative retained earnings (RETE) of $2 billion and Created Market Value of $79.3 billion. The Created Market Value multiple, defined by the change in MCap for every $1 of retained earnings is exemplary at $39.99.

3.3 Satisfies four criteria of Benjamin Graham

- The P/E of 22.5 is 0.4 times the highest average P/E of 57.7 in the last five years. This is a value criterion, according to Benjamin Graham who described as a value criterion "A P/E ratio down to less than four-tenth of the highest average P/E ratio the stock attained in the most recent five years".

-""Earnings growth over the most recent ten years of 7% compounded - that is a doubling of earnings in a five-year period""; the earnings per share have more than doubled in the last 5 years.

-""Stability of growth in earnings over the last 5 years, defined as no more than two declines of 5% or greater in year-end earnings""; there have been 2 declines in earnings in the last 5 years.

Fri Apr 24, 2026: Duke Energy Secures 20-Year License for Robinson Nuclear Plant

Duke Energy Corporation has received a significant boost with the U.S. Nuclear Regulatory Commission's (NRC) decision to grant a 20-year extension for the operating license of its Robinson Nuclear Plant. This renewal allows the facility to continue producing energy until the year 2050, a crucial development for the utility giant. The announcement was made on April 23rd, 2026, and signifies Duke Energy's commitment to providing stable and sustainable energy to the region.

The Robinson Nuclear Plant, located in Hartsville, South Carolina, plays a vital role in Duke Energy's operations, supplying electricity to approximately 570,000 households. This license extension not only secures the plant's operational future but also aligns with Duke Energy's broader strategy of maintaining a diverse energy portfolio. The company is focused on integrating nuclear, natural gas, and renewable energy sources to meet the growing energy demands of its 8 million customers across various states, including the Carolinas, Indiana, Florida, Ohio, and Kentucky.

Duke Energy operates within the regulated utilities sector, boasting a market capitalization of approximately $99.64 billion. The company is segmented into electric utilities and infrastructure, gas utilities and infrastructure, and commercial renewables. Currently, the company's P/E ratio stands at 20.26, which is close to its five-year median, indicating that the stock may be fairly valued in the current market context. Investors are encouraged to consider this valuation alongside other financial metrics when evaluating the stock's potential.

The GF Score(TradeMark) for Duke Energy is 77 out of 100, reflecting strong performance across key metrics that can lead to higher long-term returns. The company's profitability rank is notably strong at 7 out of 10, underscoring its operational efficiency. However, concerns regarding financial strength persist, with a rating of just 3 out of 10, suggesting potential issues related to debt levels and overall financial health. Investors should carefully weigh these factors when considering Duke Energy as part of their investment portfolio.

In recent months, insider trading activity has raised eyebrows, with insiders selling $5.6 million worth of shares and no recorded insider buying. This trend may lead to questions about insider confidence in the company's future performance. Overall, Duke Energy presents a mixed outlook for investors. While the recent license renewal for the Robinson Nuclear Plant strengthens its operational foundation, its financial challenges could impact long-term growth. Investors are encouraged to explore further insights about Duke Energy and its current market position.

Recent News 2 of 3, Source: HourlyBusinessNews, 271 words

Wed Apr 22, 2026: Duke Energy Honored with Arbor Day Foundation’s Tree Line USA Award for 20th Year

Duke Energy Corporation has received national recognition for its tree care practices for the 20th consecutive year. The Arbor Day Foundation named Duke Energy Florida a 'Tree Line USA' utility, citing the company's rigorous tree pruning standards, comprehensive employee training, and public education efforts aimed at reducing power outages and safeguarding electrical infrastructure.

On 22nd April 2026, the Arbor Day Foundation lauded Duke Energy for meeting industry standards for tree care, supporting tree planting initiatives, and engaging in annual arbor education programs. The designation reflects Duke Energy's commitment to sustainable practices, as well as its proactive approach to maintaining the health of urban trees in proximity to electrical lines.

One of Duke Energy's ongoing initiatives includes an annual free tree giveaway for customers through the Arbor Day Foundation's Energy-Saving Trees program. In 2026, the company distributed 1,000 trees to Florida customers, bringing the total number of free trees given away statewide to over 16,000 since 2017. These trees are shipped directly to customers with detailed planting instructions designed to encourage proper placement away from power lines.

Company officials noted that proper tree placement is essential for conserving energy and minimizing the risk of outages during storm and hurricane seasons. Duke Energy also conducts year-round vegetation maintenance near power lines and ramps up these efforts ahead of hurricane season, ensuring a reliable power supply for its customers.

Additionally, Duke Energy reminds customers not to trim trees near power lines themselves and encourages them to contact Sunshine 811 before digging near underground utilities. More information about responsible tree planting near electrical equipment can be found through Duke Energy's Right Tree Right Place program.

Recent News 3 of 3, Source: HourlyBusinessNews, 395 words

Wed Apr 22, 2026: Duke Energy Foundation Launches America250 Grants for South Carolina Green Spaces

GREENVILLE, S.C.– To strengthen communities across South Carolina as the nation approaches its 250th anniversary, Duke Energy Foundation is awarding $275,000 to nonprofit organizations through a first round of funding as part of its America250 initiative. This funding, announced on 22nd April 2026, celebrates Earth Day and aims to enhance local parks, tree planting efforts, and trail maintenance projects throughout the Palmetto State.

The grants, which form part of a total investment exceeding $550,000 in South Carolina, are aimed at revitalizing parks and shared green spaces, supporting tree planting, and litter prevention initiatives. "As we look back on our country's 250-year anniversary, it's an opportunity to reflect on our nation's history while working to support the next generation," said Tim Pearson, Duke Energy South Carolina president. "We are proud to partner with local organizations who can help care for the Palmetto State now, so future generations can enjoy its beauty."

Duke Energy Foundation has awarded a total of 11 grants in this first round of funding. Notable recipients include the South Carolina Wildlife Federation, Foothills Trail Conservancy, and the Upstate Greenways & Trails Alliance. Each organization is expected to utilize the funding to enhance local environments and foster community engagement in conservation efforts. For instance, Upstate Greenways & Trails Alliance plans to create a new trail that highlights the scenic beauty of Lake Keowee while educating visitors about the Revolutionary War history in the area.

The positive responses from grant recipients highlight the importance of such initiatives. Matt Flege, Executive Director of Upstate Greenways & Trails Alliance, expressed gratitude for the support, stating, "The America250 grant will allow us to create a new trail showcasing the beauty of Lake Keowee to South Carolina residents and visitors alike." Similarly, Susan H. Wild, Chair of Sumter Green, emphasized how the investment strengthens their commitment to beautifying the community, ensuring that efforts made today will benefit future generations.

These grants mark the first awards under Duke Energy Foundation's America250 initiative, which represents a commitment of over $1 million towards community-driven projects across the company's six-state service territories. Additional grant recipients will be announced later this spring, including initiatives focused on veterans' workforce pathways and enhancing access to history and civics education. Duke Energy Foundation continues to provide nearly $30 million annually in philanthropic support to the communities it serves, reflecting its dedication to environmental stewardship and community development.

urn:contentItem:6JH0-VC63-RX36-R3J2-00000-00CROWLEY MARITIME - PEER NEWS AND THE RECENT UPDATES2026-05-04T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:36:34ZLexisNexisSTOCKCROWLEY MARITIME - PEER NEWS AND THE RECENT UPDATES

DASHBOARD: CROWLEY MARITIME

PermID4295904276Websitehttp://www.crowley.com/IndustryMaritime & Shipping Logistics

ACTIVITIES:

Crowley Maritime Corporation, founded in 1892, is a family-owned logistics and maritime services company headquartered in Jacksonville, Florida. Employing over 5,300 people, it operates in shipping, energy, and supply chain management, generating over $2 billion annually. Crowley provides ocean transport, LNG distribution, and logistics for government and commercial clients. In 2024, it expanded renewable energy logistics, supporting offshore wind projects. Its culture emphasizes safety, innovation, and sustainability, with robust employee training. Competing with Maersk, Crowley stands out for its U.S.-focused operations and government contracts. Recent initiatives include decarbonization efforts and digital tracking systems, aligning with green logistics trends.

+ CROWLEY MARITIME [3 News That Matters]

+ CROWLEY MARITIME SECURES PERMIT TO STUDY FUEL TERMINAL AT PORT MCKENZIE

Crowley Maritime has secured permission to study the development of a large fuel terminal at Port McKenzie, a move that could broaden the company's footprint in Alaska's Matanuska-Susitna Borough. The permit, granted for a comprehensive feasibility study, was announced on January 7, 2026, as part of Crowley's plan to explore a fuel-terminal complex serving both marine and land operations.If realized, the terminal would bolster the local fuel supply chain and support regional industries amid rising demand. By leveraging Port McKenzie's strategic location, Crowley aims to streamline operations and attract other businesses to the area, potentially marking a milestone for future investment and growth in the borough. Crowley now proceeds with stakeholder engagement and detailed feasibility work.

+ CROWLEY MARITIME RESTRUCTURES LEADERSHIP TO ACCELERATE GROWTH IN SHIPPING AND ENERGY

Crowley Maritime announced on January 6, 2026 a strategic consolidation of its business structure, creating two divisions: Shipping and Logistics led by Executive Vice President and Division President James C. Fowler, and Energy led by Executive Vice President and Division President Kollin Fencil. The move is intended to boost efficiency, deliver greater value to customers and position the company for accelerated growth across its shipping, logistics and energy activities.The Shipping and Logistics Division brings together Crowley's logistics, land transportation and global ship management operations to serve commercial and government customers with ocean shipping, inland trucking, multimodal transportation and warehousing. Fowler said the new structure will enable cohesive management of the entire transportation value chain and drive growth through safe, efficient supply-chain solutions.The Energy Division consolidates Crowley Fuels in Alaska, LNG services and advanced energy solutions. Fencil noted the reorganization will enable Crowley to innovate and scale more effectively in traditional and emerging energy markets, reinforcing the company's long-standing support of diverse energy needs across the United States. Since joining Crowley as senior vice presidents in 2023, Fowler and Fencil have helped improve financial performance and operational efficiency. Chief Operating Officer Megan Davidson said the realignment will accelerate decision-making and improve the company's ability to respond to market demands, supporting Crowley's position as a leader in maritime, energy and logistics.

+ CROWLEY AND SEACOR FORGE FAIRWATER HOLDINGS TO UNITE JONES ACT TANKER FLEET

Crowley Maritime and SEACOR Holdings, via its Seabulk Tankers unit, have formed a joint venture named Fairwater Holdings to integrate their liquid energy and chemical transport assets and to operate as an independent U.S. Jones Act service provider. The agreement was finalized on January 2, 2026.Fairwater will combine the strengths of both companies to offer a range of transportation solutions across the petroleum and chemical trades, along with third-party ship management services. The venture will oversee a fleet of 20 ocean-going articulated tug-barges and 11 tankers, largely under long-term charters, and will also provide crewing and technical management for 21 additional vessels owned by third parties.Daniel Thorogood, currently CEO of Seabulk, will become Fairwater's CEO after the deal closes. Tom Crowley, chairman and CEO of Crowley Maritime, said the partnership aligns with Crowley's growth strategy and will strengthen its leadership in the U.S. maritime market. Operations are expected to begin after customary closing conditions and regulatory approvals, with Fairwater set to commence in the first quarter of 2024.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ CMA CGM LOGISTICS [3 News That Matters]

+ CMA CGM REFLAGS FIFTH VESSEL IN INDIA TO BOOST SERVICE EFFICIENCY

MUMBAI: CMA CGM Group announced the reflagging of its fifth container vessel in India, a move to enhance service efficiency on key routes. The reflagging occurred on the 24th of April 2026, underscoring CMACGM's long-standing investment and commitment to India as it expands its Indian-flag operations.The Vila do Conde, a 2,592 TEU vessel, has been reflagged to the Indian flag, aligning with a government push to increase domestic-flag tonnage. CMACGM has already reflagged five vessels and plans a sixth by the end of May as part of its expansion strategy. Earlier this year, the group signed a $350 million deal with Cochin Shipyard for six dual-fuel vessels and outlined a $300 million investment in Maharashtra's logistics infrastructure. The company is also exploring inland waterways and has discussed using Gujarat International Finance Tec-City, or GIFT City, as a vessel-registration hub to bolster maritime security and competitiveness.

+ CMA CGM ADVANCES GEMALINK TERMINAL EXPANSION TO BOLSTER VIETNAM TRADE INFRASTRUCTURE

CMA CGM Group has launched Phase 2 of expansion works at the Gemalink container terminal in Cai Mep port, southern Vietnam, with work starting on 20th April 2026. The project aims to strengthen CMACGM's port capacity in Southeast Asia and support sustainable supply chains across the region. Upon completion, Gemalink's capacity is expected to nearly double from 1.7 million TEUs to about 3 million TEUs, with full operation anticipated by 2027. The expansion includes extending the quay length by 450 meters in two segments, expanding the container yard from 32 to 44 hectares, and adding five new Ship-to-Shore cranes to lift the fleet from eight to thirteen. Gemalink has operated at full capacity since opening in 2021, handling 1.7 million TEUs annually and becoming a core node in CMACGM's Southeast Asia logistics network along key maritime routes. CMACGM's Christine Cabau Woehrel, Executive Vice President of CMA CGM Group, noted that the project underscores CMACGM's long-term partnership with Gemadept and aligns with Vietnam's role in global supply chains.

+ CMA CGM UPGRADES TRANSATLANTIC PAD SERVICE, EXTENDING DIRECT CORK-U.S. CONNECTIVITY

Effective from 28th April 2026, CMA CGM Group is expanding its Transatlantic PAD service, reinforcing Cork's role in Ireland's trade with the United States and expanding access to growing Oceania markets.Cork will gain a direct weekly connection to New York and Savannah, with transit times of about nine days to New York and eleven days to Savannah. The upgrade includes an additional vessel and optimized port rotations designed to improve schedule reliability and supply chain predictability.Key features of the enhanced PAD service include direct calls at Dunkerque and Zeebrugge, which are not available on other routes, and seamless integration with CMACGM's Northern Europe network linking Le Havre and Rotterdam for efficient feedering to Baltic and Scandinavian markets. In the United States, the service leverages CMA CGM Port Liberty terminals to provide priority berthing and bolster reliability amid ongoing congestion.The service supports a broad range of commodities, including pharmaceuticals, beverages, and general cargo, making it attractive for high value and time sensitive shipments and offering a viable alternative to air freight. The addition of Cork gives Irish exporters and importers faster, more predictable access to major U.S. markets, while CMA CGM has a long-standing presence in Ireland with three ports and up to six weekly sailings, connecting to more than 420 ports worldwide and supporting sectors such as pharmaceuticals, agri-food, and manufacturing.

value.Title: CROWLEY MARITIME - INDUSTRY DEVELOPMENTS INCLUDING CMA CGM LOGISTICS SA AND PEERS

urn:contentItem:6JH1-B483-RVD8-2280-00000-00CROWLEY MARITIME - INDUSTRY DEVELOPMENTS INCLUDING CMA CGM LOGISTICS SA AND PEERS2026-05-04T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:36:34ZLexisNexisSTOCKCROWLEY MARITIME - INDUSTRY DEVELOPMENTS INCLUDING CMA CGM LOGISTICS SA AND PEERS

DASHBOARD: CROWLEY MARITIME

PermID4295904276Websitehttp://www.crowley.com/IndustryMaritime & Shipping Logistics

ACTIVITIES:

Crowley Maritime Corporation, founded in 1892, is a family-owned logistics and maritime services company headquartered in Jacksonville, Florida. Employing over 5,300 people, it operates in shipping, energy, and supply chain management, generating over $2 billion annually. Crowley provides ocean transport, LNG distribution, and logistics for government and commercial clients. In 2024, it expanded renewable energy logistics, supporting offshore wind projects. Its culture emphasizes safety, innovation, and sustainability, with robust employee training. Competing with Maersk, Crowley stands out for its U.S.-focused operations and government contracts. Recent initiatives include decarbonization efforts and digital tracking systems, aligning with green logistics trends.

+ CROWLEY MARITIME [3 News That Matters]

+ CROWLEY MARITIME ADVANCES ALASKA GROWTH WITH PORT MCKENZIE FUEL TERMINAL FEASIBILITY

Crowley Maritime has announced a plan to conduct a feasibility study for a potential fuel terminal at Port McKenzie, signaling a broader push to expand its Alaska footprint. The permit was granted on January 7, 2026, as rising regional activity underscores demand for improved logistics and transportation capabilities.Officials say the terminal would provide a reliable fuel-supply chain for local marine and land operations, supporting growing commercial activity in the Matanuska-Susitna Borough and the broader Alaska economy.Crowley's strategy centers on leveraging Port McKenzie's location to enhance logistics and attract other tenants. If developed, the project could streamline Crowley's operations and help catalyze additional investment in the area.With the permit in place, Crowley moves forward with feasibility assessments and engagement with local stakeholders, marking a potential milestone for the company and for Port McKenzie as they explore future growth opportunities.

+ CROWLEY MARITIME ANNOUNCES DUAL-DIVISION REALIGNMENT TO ENHANCE OPERATIONAL AGILITY

Crowley Maritime announced on January 6, 2026 the formation of two new divisions—Shipping and Logistics led by James C. Fowler, and Energy led by Kollin Fencil—as part of a strategic consolidation to improve execution and accelerate growth across its shipping, logistics and energy activities.The Shipping and Logistics Division combines Crowley's logistics, land transportation and global ship management to serve commercial and government customers with ocean shipping, inland trucking, multimodal transportation and warehousing.Fowler said the reorganized structure will enable cohesive management of the entire transportation value chain, driving growth through safe and efficient supply-chain solutions.The Energy Division consolidates Crowley Fuels in Alaska, LNG services and advanced energy solutions, enabling greater innovation and scale in both traditional and emerging energy markets.Fencil noted the realignment will help Crowley meet evolving customer demands for traditional fuels and advanced energy solutions, and the leaders have delivered improvements in financial performance and operational efficiency since joining the company in 2023. Chief Operating Officer Megan Davidson said the realignment will speed decision-making and improve the company's ability to respond to market dynamics, reinforcing Crowley's status as a leader in maritime, energy and logistics. Crowley is privately held and U.S.-owned, with a footprint spanning 36 nations and territories and more than 130 years in business.

+ FAIRWATER HOLDINGS EMERGES AS NEW JONES ACT SERVICE PROVIDER FROM CROWLEY-SEACOR ALLIANCE

Crowley Maritime and SEACOR Holdings have created Fairwater Holdings, a new joint venture intended to integrate their liquid energy and chemical transportation assets and to serve as an independent U.S. Jones Act service provider.The venture will combine the strengths of Crowley and SEACOR to offer transportation solutions across the petroleum and chemical trades, plus third-party ship management.Fairwater will manage a fleet of 20 ocean-going articulated tug-barges and 11 tankers, largely under long-term charters, and will provide crewing and technical management for an additional 21 vessels owned by third parties.Daniel Thorogood, currently CEO of Seabulk, will take the helm as CEO of Fairwater once the deal closes.Crowley's Tom Crowley described Seabulk as an ideal partner with a proven track record, adding the venture supports Crowley's growth strategy and strengthens its position as the largest American-flag petroleum and chemical tank vessel fleet, with capacity exceeding 12 million barrels.The companies expect to begin operations after the customary closing conditions and regulatory approvals, with Fairwater slated to commence in the first quarter of 2024.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ CMA CGM LOGISTICS [3 News That Matters]

+ CMA CGM EXPANDS INDIAN FLAG REGISTRY WITH FIFTH VESSEL

MUMBAI: CMA CGM Group announced the reflagging of its fifth container vessel in India, saying the move will streamline services on key routes. The company noted the reflagging occurred on the 24th of April 2026, reinforcing its long-term commitment to India.The reflagging changes the Vila do Conde's registration to the Indian flag and involves a 2,592 TEU capacity vessel.This effort fits a broader government push to grow an Indian-flag fleet, with Prime Minister Modi emphasizing self-sufficiency in shipping.CMA CGM has already reflagged five vessels since last year and plans a sixth by the end of May.Earlier this year, the company ordered six dual-fuel vessels from Cochin Shipyard for about $350 million and announced a planned $300 million investment in Maharashtra's logistics infrastructure. Executives are also exploring inland waterways and proposed using Gujarat International Finance Tec-City, or GIFT City, as a vessel-registration hub to strengthen maritime security and competitiveness.

+ CMACGM'S GEMALINK EXPANSION SET TO DOUBLE VIETNAM PORT CAPACITY BY 2027

CMA CGM Group has announced Phase 2 of the Gemalink terminal expansion in Cai Mep port, southern Vietnam, with the work beginning on 20th April 2026. The move reinforces CMA CGM's commitment to expanding port capacity in Southeast Asia and supporting robust, sustainable supply chains in the region. When finished, capacity will nearly double from 1.7 million TEUs to around 3 million TEUs, with full operation expected by 2027. The plan adds 450 meters of quay in two segments, expands the container yard from 32 to 44 hectares, and introduces five additional Ship-to-Shore cranes, bringing the total to thirteen. Gemalink has quickly established itself as one of the region's most efficient terminals since its 2021 opening, currently operating at full capacity and handling 1.7 million TEUs annually. Its location along key trade lanes makes it a strategic hub within CMACGM's network in Vung Tau. Christine Cabau Woehrel, CMA CGM Group Executive Vice President, said the expansion reflects a long-term partnership with Gemadept and aligns with Vietnam's strategy to become a leading maritime logistics hub in Southeast Asia by 2050.

+ CMA CGM ENHANCES TRANSATLANTIC OFFERING WITH DIRECT CORK CALLS TO NEW YORK AND SAVANNAH

CMA CGM Group has announced enhancements to its Transatlantic PAD service, adding Cork as a direct weekly link to New York and Savannah, effective 28th April 2026.The changes include deployment of an additional vessel and optimized port rotations to improve schedule reliability and overall predictability for supply chains relying on these routes.The PAD service now features direct calls at Dunkerque and Zeebrugge, a distinguishing feature not offered by other services, and connects with CMA CGM's Northern Europe network including Le Havre and Rotterdam to facilitate feedering to Baltic and Scandinavian markets.In the United States, the service benefits from CMA CGM Port Liberty terminals, providing priority berthing to help relieve port congestion and bolster service reliability.The PAD service is capable of handling pharmaceuticals, beverages, and general cargo, appealing to time sensitive and high value shipments and offering a competitive alternative to air freight. The Cork addition strengthens Ireland's access to major U.S. markets, complementing CMA CGM's two-decade presence in Ireland across three ports and up to six weekly sailings, linking the country to more than 420 ports worldwide and supporting key sectors such as pharmaceuticals, agri-food, and manufacturing.

Fri Apr 24, 2026: Duke Energy Secures 20-Year License for Robinson Nuclear Plant

Duke Energy Corporation has received a significant boost with the U.S. Nuclear Regulatory Commission's (NRC) decision to grant a 20-year extension for the operating license of its Robinson Nuclear Plant. This renewal allows the facility to continue producing energy until the year 2050, a crucial development for the utility giant. The announcement was made on April 23rd, 2026, and signifies Duke Energy's commitment to providing stable and sustainable energy to the region.

The Robinson Nuclear Plant, located in Hartsville, South Carolina, plays a vital role in Duke Energy's operations, supplying electricity to approximately 570,000 households. This license extension not only secures the plant's operational future but also aligns with Duke Energy's broader strategy of maintaining a diverse energy portfolio. The company is focused on integrating nuclear, natural gas, and renewable energy sources to meet the growing energy demands of its 8 million customers across various states, including the Carolinas, Indiana, Florida, Ohio, and Kentucky.

Duke Energy operates within the regulated utilities sector, boasting a market capitalization of approximately $99.64 billion. The company is segmented into electric utilities and infrastructure, gas utilities and infrastructure, and commercial renewables. Currently, the company's P/E ratio stands at 20.26, which is close to its five-year median, indicating that the stock may be fairly valued in the current market context. Investors are encouraged to consider this valuation alongside other financial metrics when evaluating the stock's potential.

The GF Score(TradeMark) for Duke Energy is 77 out of 100, reflecting strong performance across key metrics that can lead to higher long-term returns. The company's profitability rank is notably strong at 7 out of 10, underscoring its operational efficiency. However, concerns regarding financial strength persist, with a rating of just 3 out of 10, suggesting potential issues related to debt levels and overall financial health. Investors should carefully weigh these factors when considering Duke Energy as part of their investment portfolio.

In recent months, insider trading activity has raised eyebrows, with insiders selling $5.6 million worth of shares and no recorded insider buying. This trend may lead to questions about insider confidence in the company's future performance. Overall, Duke Energy presents a mixed outlook for investors. While the recent license renewal for the Robinson Nuclear Plant strengthens its operational foundation, its financial challenges could impact long-term growth. Investors are encouraged to explore further insights about Duke Energy and its current market position.

Recent News 2 of 3, Source: HourlyBusinessNews, 395 words

Wed Apr 22, 2026: Duke Energy Foundation Launches America250 Grants for South Carolina Green Spaces

GREENVILLE, S.C.– To strengthen communities across South Carolina as the nation approaches its 250th anniversary, Duke Energy Foundation is awarding $275,000 to nonprofit organizations through a first round of funding as part of its America250 initiative. This funding, announced on 22nd April 2026, celebrates Earth Day and aims to enhance local parks, tree planting efforts, and trail maintenance projects throughout the Palmetto State.

The grants, which form part of a total investment exceeding $550,000 in South Carolina, are aimed at revitalizing parks and shared green spaces, supporting tree planting, and litter prevention initiatives. "As we look back on our country's 250-year anniversary, it's an opportunity to reflect on our nation's history while working to support the next generation," said Tim Pearson, Duke Energy South Carolina president. "We are proud to partner with local organizations who can help care for the Palmetto State now, so future generations can enjoy its beauty."

Duke Energy Foundation has awarded a total of 11 grants in this first round of funding. Notable recipients include the South Carolina Wildlife Federation, Foothills Trail Conservancy, and the Upstate Greenways & Trails Alliance. Each organization is expected to utilize the funding to enhance local environments and foster community engagement in conservation efforts. For instance, Upstate Greenways & Trails Alliance plans to create a new trail that highlights the scenic beauty of Lake Keowee while educating visitors about the Revolutionary War history in the area.

The positive responses from grant recipients highlight the importance of such initiatives. Matt Flege, Executive Director of Upstate Greenways & Trails Alliance, expressed gratitude for the support, stating, "The America250 grant will allow us to create a new trail showcasing the beauty of Lake Keowee to South Carolina residents and visitors alike." Similarly, Susan H. Wild, Chair of Sumter Green, emphasized how the investment strengthens their commitment to beautifying the community, ensuring that efforts made today will benefit future generations.

These grants mark the first awards under Duke Energy Foundation's America250 initiative, which represents a commitment of over $1 million towards community-driven projects across the company's six-state service territories. Additional grant recipients will be announced later this spring, including initiatives focused on veterans' workforce pathways and enhancing access to history and civics education. Duke Energy Foundation continues to provide nearly $30 million annually in philanthropic support to the communities it serves, reflecting its dedication to environmental stewardship and community development.

Recent News 3 of 3, Source: HourlyBusinessNews, 271 words

Wed Apr 22, 2026: Duke Energy Honored with Arbor Day Foundation’s Tree Line USA Award for 20th Year

Duke Energy Corporation has received national recognition for its tree care practices for the 20th consecutive year. The Arbor Day Foundation named Duke Energy Florida a 'Tree Line USA' utility, citing the company's rigorous tree pruning standards, comprehensive employee training, and public education efforts aimed at reducing power outages and safeguarding electrical infrastructure.

On 22nd April 2026, the Arbor Day Foundation lauded Duke Energy for meeting industry standards for tree care, supporting tree planting initiatives, and engaging in annual arbor education programs. The designation reflects Duke Energy's commitment to sustainable practices, as well as its proactive approach to maintaining the health of urban trees in proximity to electrical lines.

One of Duke Energy's ongoing initiatives includes an annual free tree giveaway for customers through the Arbor Day Foundation's Energy-Saving Trees program. In 2026, the company distributed 1,000 trees to Florida customers, bringing the total number of free trees given away statewide to over 16,000 since 2017. These trees are shipped directly to customers with detailed planting instructions designed to encourage proper placement away from power lines.

Company officials noted that proper tree placement is essential for conserving energy and minimizing the risk of outages during storm and hurricane seasons. Duke Energy also conducts year-round vegetation maintenance near power lines and ramps up these efforts ahead of hurricane season, ensuring a reliable power supply for its customers.

Additionally, Duke Energy reminds customers not to trim trees near power lines themselves and encourages them to contact Sunshine 811 before digging near underground utilities. More information about responsible tree planting near electrical equipment can be found through Duke Energy's Right Tree Right Place program.

SECTION 2 TODAY'S BULLISH SIGNALS

2.1 Uptrend

Today's Volatility:

The 4% discount to 12-month high of $134.49 against the 21% premium to 12-month low of $107.42 suggests the stock continues to push for new highs.

Today its volatility (highest price minus lowest price/lowest price) of 2.9% was 1.9 times the average daily volatility of 1.6%, up from 1.3% on Wednesday and 1.2% on Tuesday. A rise in the price on high volatility is a bullish signal.

Price/Moving Average Price of 1.06:

- The Price/MAP 200 for Duke Energy Corporation is 1.06. Being higher than 1 is a bullish indicator. The stock is trading above both its MAPs and the 50-day MAP of $132.99 is higher than the 200-day MAP of $122.41, a second bullish indicator. The 200-day MAP has increased to $122.41, a third bullish indicator.

Past Quarter:

- In the last three months the stock has hit a new 52-week high twelve times, pointing to a significant uptrend.

- The shares had their best quarter in a year since the quarter ended April 30, 2025.

2.2 Other Bullish Signals

- Return on Equity of 9.0% versus sector average of 5.8%.

MCap/Total Assets:

- Tobin's Q Ratio, defined as MCap divided by Total Assets, is 0.5. Compared with the rest of the market the stock is undervalued.

- Net profit margin has averaged 14.4% in the last 3 years. This is better than the sector average of 11.8% and suggests a high margin of safety.

- As per the Du Pont analysis, Return on Equity of 9.0% is better than sector average of 5.8%. This is computed as net profit margin of 15.2% times asset turnover [sales/assets] of 0.16 times leverage factor [total assets/shareholders' equity] of 3.6.

SECTION 3 ONGOING BULLISH PARAMETERS

3.1 Rule of 40

The stock scores a favorable score exceeding 40 when using the Rule of 40 (Revenue Growth plus EBITDA margin). Y.o.y revenue growth of 4%, EBITDA margin is 36.2% and the sum of the two 40.7% needs to exceed 40%.

Source: Duke Energy Corporation's, 10-K filing dated February 27, 2025, available at the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission website: https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/17797/000132616025000072/duk-20241231.htm and Duke Energy Corporation's website: https://investors.duke-energy.com/overview/default.aspx. company (Duke Energy Corporation), Ticker(DUK)

3.2 Created Market Value [CMV] past 17 yrs of $80.2 billion

- Market Capitalization has increased by $82.2 billion from $18.4 billion to $100.7 billion in the last 17 years. This increase comprises cumulative retained earnings (RETE) of $2 billion and Created Market Value of $80.2 billion. The Created Market Value multiple, defined by the change in MCap for every $1 of retained earnings is exemplary at $40.44.

3.3 Satisfies four criteria of Benjamin Graham

- The P/E of 22.7 is 0.4 times the highest average P/E of 57.7 in the last five years. This is a value criterion, according to Benjamin Graham who described as a value criterion "A P/E ratio down to less than four-tenth of the highest average P/E ratio the stock attained in the most recent five years".

-""Earnings growth over the most recent ten years of 7% compounded - that is a doubling of earnings in a five-year period""; the earnings per share have more than doubled in the last 5 years.

-""Stability of growth in earnings over the last 5 years, defined as no more than two declines of 5% or greater in year-end earnings""; there have been 2 declines in earnings in the last 5 years.

Source: Duke Energy Corporation's, 10-K filing dated February 27, 2025, available at the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission website: https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/17797/000132616025000072/duk-20241231.htm and Duke Energy Corporation's website: https://investors.duke-energy.com/overview/default.aspx. company (Duke Energy Corporation), Ticker(DUK)

3.4 Satisfies 6 out of 9 criteria of Joseph Piotroski [pass mark 5]:

- Positive net income.

- Positive operating cashflow.

- Return on Assets improvement [from 2.4% to 2.5%].

- Good quality of earnings [operating cashflow exceeds net income].

- Improvement in gross margin [from 98.0% to 98.1%].

- Improvement in asset turnover [growth in revenue of 6.2% exceeded growth in assets of 5.3%].

But does not meet the following 3 criteria of Joseph Piotroski:

- Improvement in long-term debt to total assets.

- Improvement in current ratio.

- Total shares on issue unchanged (or reduction in total shares on issue).

Duke Energy Corporation is ranked number 1 out of 32 listed utility companies in the United States with a market capitalization of $100.7 billion.

Within its sector it has a relatively low P/E/G of 0.37.

It has a moderate relative ROE of 9.0% and ROA of 2.5%. The company paid a dividend of $3.19 in the last twelve months. The dividend yield is moderate at 3.3%. Finally, its earnings growth in the past 12 months has been a comparatively high 61.3%.

Fri Apr 24, 2026: Duke Energy Secures 20-Year License for Robinson Nuclear Plant

Duke Energy Corporation has received a significant boost with the U.S. Nuclear Regulatory Commission's (NRC) decision to grant a 20-year extension for the operating license of its Robinson Nuclear Plant. This renewal allows the facility to continue producing energy until the year 2050, a crucial development for the utility giant. The announcement was made on April 23rd, 2026, and signifies Duke Energy's commitment to providing stable and sustainable energy to the region.

The Robinson Nuclear Plant, located in Hartsville, South Carolina, plays a vital role in Duke Energy's operations, supplying electricity to approximately 570,000 households. This license extension not only secures the plant's operational future but also aligns with Duke Energy's broader strategy of maintaining a diverse energy portfolio. The company is focused on integrating nuclear, natural gas, and renewable energy sources to meet the growing energy demands of its 8 million customers across various states, including the Carolinas, Indiana, Florida, Ohio, and Kentucky.

Duke Energy operates within the regulated utilities sector, boasting a market capitalization of approximately $99.64 billion. The company is segmented into electric utilities and infrastructure, gas utilities and infrastructure, and commercial renewables. Currently, the company's P/E ratio stands at 20.26, which is close to its five-year median, indicating that the stock may be fairly valued in the current market context. Investors are encouraged to consider this valuation alongside other financial metrics when evaluating the stock's potential.

The GF Score(TradeMark) for Duke Energy is 77 out of 100, reflecting strong performance across key metrics that can lead to higher long-term returns. The company's profitability rank is notably strong at 7 out of 10, underscoring its operational efficiency. However, concerns regarding financial strength persist, with a rating of just 3 out of 10, suggesting potential issues related to debt levels and overall financial health. Investors should carefully weigh these factors when considering Duke Energy as part of their investment portfolio.

In recent months, insider trading activity has raised eyebrows, with insiders selling $5.6 million worth of shares and no recorded insider buying. This trend may lead to questions about insider confidence in the company's future performance. Overall, Duke Energy presents a mixed outlook for investors. While the recent license renewal for the Robinson Nuclear Plant strengthens its operational foundation, its financial challenges could impact long-term growth. Investors are encouraged to explore further insights about Duke Energy and its current market position.

Recent News 2 of 3, Source: HourlyBusinessNews, 395 words

Wed Apr 22, 2026: Duke Energy Foundation Launches America250 Grants for South Carolina Green Spaces

GREENVILLE, S.C.– To strengthen communities across South Carolina as the nation approaches its 250th anniversary, Duke Energy Foundation is awarding $275,000 to nonprofit organizations through a first round of funding as part of its America250 initiative. This funding, announced on 22nd April 2026, celebrates Earth Day and aims to enhance local parks, tree planting efforts, and trail maintenance projects throughout the Palmetto State.

The grants, which form part of a total investment exceeding $550,000 in South Carolina, are aimed at revitalizing parks and shared green spaces, supporting tree planting, and litter prevention initiatives. "As we look back on our country's 250-year anniversary, it's an opportunity to reflect on our nation's history while working to support the next generation," said Tim Pearson, Duke Energy South Carolina president. "We are proud to partner with local organizations who can help care for the Palmetto State now, so future generations can enjoy its beauty."

Duke Energy Foundation has awarded a total of 11 grants in this first round of funding. Notable recipients include the South Carolina Wildlife Federation, Foothills Trail Conservancy, and the Upstate Greenways & Trails Alliance. Each organization is expected to utilize the funding to enhance local environments and foster community engagement in conservation efforts. For instance, Upstate Greenways & Trails Alliance plans to create a new trail that highlights the scenic beauty of Lake Keowee while educating visitors about the Revolutionary War history in the area.

The positive responses from grant recipients highlight the importance of such initiatives. Matt Flege, Executive Director of Upstate Greenways & Trails Alliance, expressed gratitude for the support, stating, "The America250 grant will allow us to create a new trail showcasing the beauty of Lake Keowee to South Carolina residents and visitors alike." Similarly, Susan H. Wild, Chair of Sumter Green, emphasized how the investment strengthens their commitment to beautifying the community, ensuring that efforts made today will benefit future generations.

These grants mark the first awards under Duke Energy Foundation's America250 initiative, which represents a commitment of over $1 million towards community-driven projects across the company's six-state service territories. Additional grant recipients will be announced later this spring, including initiatives focused on veterans' workforce pathways and enhancing access to history and civics education. Duke Energy Foundation continues to provide nearly $30 million annually in philanthropic support to the communities it serves, reflecting its dedication to environmental stewardship and community development.

Recent News 3 of 3, Source: HourlyBusinessNews, 271 words

Wed Apr 22, 2026: Duke Energy Honored with Arbor Day Foundation’s Tree Line USA Award for 20th Year

Duke Energy Corporation has received national recognition for its tree care practices for the 20th consecutive year. The Arbor Day Foundation named Duke Energy Florida a 'Tree Line USA' utility, citing the company's rigorous tree pruning standards, comprehensive employee training, and public education efforts aimed at reducing power outages and safeguarding electrical infrastructure.

On 22nd April 2026, the Arbor Day Foundation lauded Duke Energy for meeting industry standards for tree care, supporting tree planting initiatives, and engaging in annual arbor education programs. The designation reflects Duke Energy's commitment to sustainable practices, as well as its proactive approach to maintaining the health of urban trees in proximity to electrical lines.

One of Duke Energy's ongoing initiatives includes an annual free tree giveaway for customers through the Arbor Day Foundation's Energy-Saving Trees program. In 2026, the company distributed 1,000 trees to Florida customers, bringing the total number of free trees given away statewide to over 16,000 since 2017. These trees are shipped directly to customers with detailed planting instructions designed to encourage proper placement away from power lines.

Company officials noted that proper tree placement is essential for conserving energy and minimizing the risk of outages during storm and hurricane seasons. Duke Energy also conducts year-round vegetation maintenance near power lines and ramps up these efforts ahead of hurricane season, ensuring a reliable power supply for its customers.

Additionally, Duke Energy reminds customers not to trim trees near power lines themselves and encourages them to contact Sunshine 811 before digging near underground utilities. More information about responsible tree planting near electrical equipment can be found through Duke Energy's Right Tree Right Place program.

SECTION 2 TODAY'S BULLISH SIGNALS

2.1 Uptrend

Today's Volatility:

The 4% discount to 12-month high of $134.49 against the 21% premium to 12-month low of $107.42 suggests the stock continues to push for new highs.

Today its volatility (highest price minus lowest price/lowest price) of 2.9% was 1.9 times the average daily volatility of 1.6%, up from 1.3% on Wednesday and 1.2% on Tuesday. A rise in the price on high volatility is a bullish signal.

Price/Moving Average Price of 1.06:

- The Price/MAP 200 for Duke Energy Corporation is 1.06. Being higher than 1 is a bullish indicator. The stock is trading above both its MAPs and the 50-day MAP of $132.99 is higher than the 200-day MAP of $122.41, a second bullish indicator. The 200-day MAP has increased to $122.41, a third bullish indicator.

Past Quarter:

- In the last three months the stock has hit a new 52-week high twelve times, pointing to a significant uptrend.

- The shares had their best quarter in a year since the quarter ended April 30, 2025.

2.2 Other Bullish Signals

- Return on Equity of 9.0% versus sector average of 5.8%.

MCap/Total Assets:

- Tobin's Q Ratio, defined as MCap divided by Total Assets, is 0.5. Compared with the rest of the market the stock is undervalued.

- Net profit margin has averaged 14.4% in the last 3 years. This is better than the sector average of 11.8% and suggests a high margin of safety.

- As per the Du Pont analysis, Return on Equity of 9.0% is better than sector average of 5.8%. This is computed as net profit margin of 15.2% times asset turnover [sales/assets] of 0.16 times leverage factor [total assets/shareholders' equity] of 3.6.

SECTION 3 ONGOING BULLISH PARAMETERS

3.1 Rule of 40

The stock scores a favorable score exceeding 40 when using the Rule of 40 (Revenue Growth plus EBITDA margin). Y.o.y revenue growth of 4%, EBITDA margin is 36.2% and the sum of the two 40.7% needs to exceed 40%.

Source: Duke Energy Corporation's, 10-K filing dated February 27, 2025, available at the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission website: https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/17797/000132616025000072/duk-20241231.htm and Duke Energy Corporation's website: https://investors.duke-energy.com/overview/default.aspx. company (Duke Energy Corporation), Ticker(DUK)

3.2 Created Market Value [CMV] past 17 yrs of $80.2 billion

- Market Capitalization has increased by $82.2 billion from $18.4 billion to $100.7 billion in the last 17 years. This increase comprises cumulative retained earnings (RETE) of $2 billion and Created Market Value of $80.2 billion. The Created Market Value multiple, defined by the change in MCap for every $1 of retained earnings is exemplary at $40.44.

3.3 Satisfies four criteria of Benjamin Graham

- The P/E of 22.7 is 0.4 times the highest average P/E of 57.7 in the last five years. This is a value criterion, according to Benjamin Graham who described as a value criterion "A P/E ratio down to less than four-tenth of the highest average P/E ratio the stock attained in the most recent five years".

-""Earnings growth over the most recent ten years of 7% compounded - that is a doubling of earnings in a five-year period""; the earnings per share have more than doubled in the last 5 years.

-""Stability of growth in earnings over the last 5 years, defined as no more than two declines of 5% or greater in year-end earnings""; there have been 2 declines in earnings in the last 5 years.

Source: Duke Energy Corporation's, 10-K filing dated February 27, 2025, available at the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission website: https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/17797/000132616025000072/duk-20241231.htm and Duke Energy Corporation's website: https://investors.duke-energy.com/overview/default.aspx. company (Duke Energy Corporation), Ticker(DUK)

3.4 Satisfies 6 out of 9 criteria of Joseph Piotroski [pass mark 5]:

- Positive net income.

- Positive operating cashflow.

- Return on Assets improvement [from 2.4% to 2.5%].

- Good quality of earnings [operating cashflow exceeds net income].

- Improvement in gross margin [from 98.0% to 98.1%].

- Improvement in asset turnover [growth in revenue of 6.2% exceeded growth in assets of 5.3%].

But does not meet the following 3 criteria of Joseph Piotroski:

- Improvement in long-term debt to total assets.

- Improvement in current ratio.

- Total shares on issue unchanged (or reduction in total shares on issue).

SECTION 5 PEER COMPARISON & RANKING OF DUK

5.1 LONG-TERM FUNDAMENTAL RANKING: 3 OUT OF 5 [5 is best]

Within its sector it has a relatively low P/E/G of 0.37.

It has a moderate relative ROE of 9.0% and ROA of 2.5%. The company paid a dividend of $3.19 in the last twelve months. The dividend yield is moderate at 3.3%. Finally, its earnings growth in the past 12 months has been a comparatively high 61.3%.

urn:contentItem:6JGB-S5D3-RTDH-2434-00000-00CROWLEY MARITIME - UPDATED NEWS FROM CMA CGM LOGISTICS SA AND PEERS2026-05-01T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:36:35ZLexisNexisSTOCKCROWLEY MARITIME - UPDATED NEWS FROM CMA CGM LOGISTICS SA AND PEERS

DASHBOARD: CROWLEY MARITIME

PermID4295904276Websitehttp://www.crowley.com/IndustryMaritime & Shipping Logistics

ACTIVITIES:

Crowley Maritime Corporation, founded in 1892, is a family-owned logistics and maritime services company headquartered in Jacksonville, Florida. Employing over 5,300 people, it operates in shipping, energy, and supply chain management, generating over $2 billion annually. Crowley provides ocean transport, LNG distribution, and logistics for government and commercial clients. In 2024, it expanded renewable energy logistics, supporting offshore wind projects. Its culture emphasizes safety, innovation, and sustainability, with robust employee training. Competing with Maersk, Crowley stands out for its U.S.-focused operations and government contracts. Recent initiatives include decarbonization efforts and digital tracking systems, aligning with green logistics trends.

+ CROWLEY MARITIME [3 News That Matters]

+ CROWLEY MARITIME SECURES PERMIT TO STUDY FUEL TERMINAL AT PORT MCKENZIE

Crowley Maritime has secured permission to study the development of a large fuel terminal at Port McKenzie, a move that could broaden the company's footprint in Alaska's Matanuska-Susitna Borough. The permit, granted for a comprehensive feasibility study, was announced on January 7, 2026, as part of Crowley's plan to explore a fuel-terminal complex serving both marine and land operations.If realized, the terminal would bolster the local fuel supply chain and support regional industries amid rising demand. By leveraging Port McKenzie's strategic location, Crowley aims to streamline operations and attract other businesses to the area, potentially marking a milestone for future investment and growth in the borough. Crowley now proceeds with stakeholder engagement and detailed feasibility work.

+ CROWLEY MARITIME RESTRUCTURES LEADERSHIP TO ACCELERATE GROWTH IN SHIPPING AND ENERGY

Crowley Maritime announced on January 6, 2026 a strategic consolidation of its business structure, creating two divisions: Shipping and Logistics led by Executive Vice President and Division President James C. Fowler, and Energy led by Executive Vice President and Division President Kollin Fencil. The move is intended to boost efficiency, deliver greater value to customers and position the company for accelerated growth across its shipping, logistics and energy activities.The Shipping and Logistics Division brings together Crowley's logistics, land transportation and global ship management operations to serve commercial and government customers with ocean shipping, inland trucking, multimodal transportation and warehousing. Fowler said the new structure will enable cohesive management of the entire transportation value chain and drive growth through safe, efficient supply-chain solutions.The Energy Division consolidates Crowley Fuels in Alaska, LNG services and advanced energy solutions. Fencil noted the reorganization will enable Crowley to innovate and scale more effectively in traditional and emerging energy markets, reinforcing the company's long-standing support of diverse energy needs across the United States. Since joining Crowley as senior vice presidents in 2023, Fowler and Fencil have helped improve financial performance and operational efficiency. Chief Operating Officer Megan Davidson said the realignment will accelerate decision-making and improve the company's ability to respond to market demands, supporting Crowley's position as a leader in maritime, energy and logistics.

+ CROWLEY AND SEACOR FORGE FAIRWATER HOLDINGS TO UNITE JONES ACT TANKER FLEET

Crowley Maritime and SEACOR Holdings, via its Seabulk Tankers unit, have formed a joint venture named Fairwater Holdings to integrate their liquid energy and chemical transport assets and to operate as an independent U.S. Jones Act service provider. The agreement was finalized on January 2, 2026.Fairwater will combine the strengths of both companies to offer a range of transportation solutions across the petroleum and chemical trades, along with third-party ship management services. The venture will oversee a fleet of 20 ocean-going articulated tug-barges and 11 tankers, largely under long-term charters, and will also provide crewing and technical management for 21 additional vessels owned by third parties.Daniel Thorogood, currently CEO of Seabulk, will become Fairwater's CEO after the deal closes. Tom Crowley, chairman and CEO of Crowley Maritime, said the partnership aligns with Crowley's growth strategy and will strengthen its leadership in the U.S. maritime market. Operations are expected to begin after customary closing conditions and regulatory approvals, with Fairwater set to commence in the first quarter of 2024.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ CMA CGM LOGISTICS [3 News That Matters]

+ CMA CGM REFLAGS FIFTH VESSEL IN INDIA TO BOOST SERVICE EFFICIENCY

MUMBAI: CMA CGM Group announced the reflagging of its fifth container vessel in India, a move to enhance service efficiency on key routes. The reflagging occurred on the 24th of April 2026, underscoring CMACGM's long-standing investment and commitment to India as it expands its Indian-flag operations.The Vila do Conde, a 2,592 TEU vessel, has been reflagged to the Indian flag, aligning with a government push to increase domestic-flag tonnage. CMACGM has already reflagged five vessels and plans a sixth by the end of May as part of its expansion strategy. Earlier this year, the group signed a $350 million deal with Cochin Shipyard for six dual-fuel vessels and outlined a $300 million investment in Maharashtra's logistics infrastructure. The company is also exploring inland waterways and has discussed using Gujarat International Finance Tec-City, or GIFT City, as a vessel-registration hub to bolster maritime security and competitiveness.

+ CMA CGM ADVANCES GEMALINK TERMINAL EXPANSION TO BOLSTER VIETNAM TRADE INFRASTRUCTURE

CMA CGM Group has launched Phase 2 of expansion works at the Gemalink container terminal in Cai Mep port, southern Vietnam, with work starting on 20th April 2026. The project aims to strengthen CMACGM's port capacity in Southeast Asia and support sustainable supply chains across the region. Upon completion, Gemalink's capacity is expected to nearly double from 1.7 million TEUs to about 3 million TEUs, with full operation anticipated by 2027. The expansion includes extending the quay length by 450 meters in two segments, expanding the container yard from 32 to 44 hectares, and adding five new Ship-to-Shore cranes to lift the fleet from eight to thirteen. Gemalink has operated at full capacity since opening in 2021, handling 1.7 million TEUs annually and becoming a core node in CMACGM's Southeast Asia logistics network along key maritime routes. CMACGM's Christine Cabau Woehrel, Executive Vice President of CMA CGM Group, noted that the project underscores CMACGM's long-term partnership with Gemadept and aligns with Vietnam's role in global supply chains.

+ CMA CGM UPGRADES TRANSATLANTIC PAD SERVICE, EXTENDING DIRECT CORK-U.S. CONNECTIVITY

Effective from 28th April 2026, CMA CGM Group is expanding its Transatlantic PAD service, reinforcing Cork's role in Ireland's trade with the United States and expanding access to growing Oceania markets.Cork will gain a direct weekly connection to New York and Savannah, with transit times of about nine days to New York and eleven days to Savannah. The upgrade includes an additional vessel and optimized port rotations designed to improve schedule reliability and supply chain predictability.Key features of the enhanced PAD service include direct calls at Dunkerque and Zeebrugge, which are not available on other routes, and seamless integration with CMACGM's Northern Europe network linking Le Havre and Rotterdam for efficient feedering to Baltic and Scandinavian markets. In the United States, the service leverages CMA CGM Port Liberty terminals to provide priority berthing and bolster reliability amid ongoing congestion.The service supports a broad range of commodities, including pharmaceuticals, beverages, and general cargo, making it attractive for high value and time sensitive shipments and offering a viable alternative to air freight. The addition of Cork gives Irish exporters and importers faster, more predictable access to major U.S. markets, while CMA CGM has a long-standing presence in Ireland with three ports and up to six weekly sailings, connecting to more than 420 ports worldwide and supporting sectors such as pharmaceuticals, agri-food, and manufacturing.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++
