urn:contentItem:6JGC-DM23-S9SW-31H0-00000-00CROWLEY MARITIME - NEWS AND TRENDS FROM CMA CGM LOGISTICS SA AND ITS PEERS2026-05-01T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:36:34ZLexisNexisSTOCKCROWLEY MARITIME - NEWS AND TRENDS FROM CMA CGM LOGISTICS SA AND ITS PEERS

DASHBOARD: CROWLEY MARITIME

PermID4295904276Websitehttp://www.crowley.com/IndustryMaritime & Shipping Logistics

ACTIVITIES:

Crowley Maritime Corporation, founded in 1892, is a family-owned logistics and maritime services company headquartered in Jacksonville, Florida. Employing over 5,300 people, it operates in shipping, energy, and supply chain management, generating over $2 billion annually. Crowley provides ocean transport, LNG distribution, and logistics for government and commercial clients. In 2024, it expanded renewable energy logistics, supporting offshore wind projects. Its culture emphasizes safety, innovation, and sustainability, with robust employee training. Competing with Maersk, Crowley stands out for its U.S.-focused operations and government contracts. Recent initiatives include decarbonization efforts and digital tracking systems, aligning with green logistics trends.

+ CROWLEY MARITIME [3 News That Matters]

+ CROWLEY MARITIME ADVANCES ALASKA GROWTH WITH PORT MCKENZIE FUEL TERMINAL FEASIBILITY

Crowley Maritime has announced a plan to conduct a feasibility study for a potential fuel terminal at Port McKenzie, signaling a broader push to expand its Alaska footprint. The permit was granted on January 7, 2026, as rising regional activity underscores demand for improved logistics and transportation capabilities.Officials say the terminal would provide a reliable fuel-supply chain for local marine and land operations, supporting growing commercial activity in the Matanuska-Susitna Borough and the broader Alaska economy.Crowley's strategy centers on leveraging Port McKenzie's location to enhance logistics and attract other tenants. If developed, the project could streamline Crowley's operations and help catalyze additional investment in the area.With the permit in place, Crowley moves forward with feasibility assessments and engagement with local stakeholders, marking a potential milestone for the company and for Port McKenzie as they explore future growth opportunities.

+ CROWLEY MARITIME ANNOUNCES DUAL-DIVISION REALIGNMENT TO ENHANCE OPERATIONAL AGILITY

Crowley Maritime announced on January 6, 2026 the formation of two new divisions—Shipping and Logistics led by James C. Fowler, and Energy led by Kollin Fencil—as part of a strategic consolidation to improve execution and accelerate growth across its shipping, logistics and energy activities.The Shipping and Logistics Division combines Crowley's logistics, land transportation and global ship management to serve commercial and government customers with ocean shipping, inland trucking, multimodal transportation and warehousing.Fowler said the reorganized structure will enable cohesive management of the entire transportation value chain, driving growth through safe and efficient supply-chain solutions.The Energy Division consolidates Crowley Fuels in Alaska, LNG services and advanced energy solutions, enabling greater innovation and scale in both traditional and emerging energy markets.Fencil noted the realignment will help Crowley meet evolving customer demands for traditional fuels and advanced energy solutions, and the leaders have delivered improvements in financial performance and operational efficiency since joining the company in 2023. Chief Operating Officer Megan Davidson said the realignment will speed decision-making and improve the company's ability to respond to market dynamics, reinforcing Crowley's status as a leader in maritime, energy and logistics. Crowley is privately held and U.S.-owned, with a footprint spanning 36 nations and territories and more than 130 years in business.

+ FAIRWATER HOLDINGS EMERGES AS NEW JONES ACT SERVICE PROVIDER FROM CROWLEY-SEACOR ALLIANCE

Crowley Maritime and SEACOR Holdings have created Fairwater Holdings, a new joint venture intended to integrate their liquid energy and chemical transportation assets and to serve as an independent U.S. Jones Act service provider.The venture will combine the strengths of Crowley and SEACOR to offer transportation solutions across the petroleum and chemical trades, plus third-party ship management.Fairwater will manage a fleet of 20 ocean-going articulated tug-barges and 11 tankers, largely under long-term charters, and will provide crewing and technical management for an additional 21 vessels owned by third parties.Daniel Thorogood, currently CEO of Seabulk, will take the helm as CEO of Fairwater once the deal closes.Crowley's Tom Crowley described Seabulk as an ideal partner with a proven track record, adding the venture supports Crowley's growth strategy and strengthens its position as the largest American-flag petroleum and chemical tank vessel fleet, with capacity exceeding 12 million barrels.The companies expect to begin operations after the customary closing conditions and regulatory approvals, with Fairwater slated to commence in the first quarter of 2024.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ CMA CGM LOGISTICS [3 News That Matters]

+ CMA CGM EXPANDS INDIAN FLAG REGISTRY WITH FIFTH VESSEL

MUMBAI: CMA CGM Group announced the reflagging of its fifth container vessel in India, saying the move will streamline services on key routes. The company noted the reflagging occurred on the 24th of April 2026, reinforcing its long-term commitment to India.The reflagging changes the Vila do Conde's registration to the Indian flag and involves a 2,592 TEU capacity vessel.This effort fits a broader government push to grow an Indian-flag fleet, with Prime Minister Modi emphasizing self-sufficiency in shipping.CMA CGM has already reflagged five vessels since last year and plans a sixth by the end of May.Earlier this year, the company ordered six dual-fuel vessels from Cochin Shipyard for about $350 million and announced a planned $300 million investment in Maharashtra's logistics infrastructure. Executives are also exploring inland waterways and proposed using Gujarat International Finance Tec-City, or GIFT City, as a vessel-registration hub to strengthen maritime security and competitiveness.

+ CMACGM'S GEMALINK EXPANSION SET TO DOUBLE VIETNAM PORT CAPACITY BY 2027

CMA CGM Group has announced Phase 2 of the Gemalink terminal expansion in Cai Mep port, southern Vietnam, with the work beginning on 20th April 2026. The move reinforces CMA CGM's commitment to expanding port capacity in Southeast Asia and supporting robust, sustainable supply chains in the region. When finished, capacity will nearly double from 1.7 million TEUs to around 3 million TEUs, with full operation expected by 2027. The plan adds 450 meters of quay in two segments, expands the container yard from 32 to 44 hectares, and introduces five additional Ship-to-Shore cranes, bringing the total to thirteen. Gemalink has quickly established itself as one of the region's most efficient terminals since its 2021 opening, currently operating at full capacity and handling 1.7 million TEUs annually. Its location along key trade lanes makes it a strategic hub within CMACGM's network in Vung Tau. Christine Cabau Woehrel, CMA CGM Group Executive Vice President, said the expansion reflects a long-term partnership with Gemadept and aligns with Vietnam's strategy to become a leading maritime logistics hub in Southeast Asia by 2050.

+ CMA CGM ENHANCES TRANSATLANTIC OFFERING WITH DIRECT CORK CALLS TO NEW YORK AND SAVANNAH

CMA CGM Group has announced enhancements to its Transatlantic PAD service, adding Cork as a direct weekly link to New York and Savannah, effective 28th April 2026.The changes include deployment of an additional vessel and optimized port rotations to improve schedule reliability and overall predictability for supply chains relying on these routes.The PAD service now features direct calls at Dunkerque and Zeebrugge, a distinguishing feature not offered by other services, and connects with CMA CGM's Northern Europe network including Le Havre and Rotterdam to facilitate feedering to Baltic and Scandinavian markets.In the United States, the service benefits from CMA CGM Port Liberty terminals, providing priority berthing to help relieve port congestion and bolster service reliability.The PAD service is capable of handling pharmaceuticals, beverages, and general cargo, appealing to time sensitive and high value shipments and offering a competitive alternative to air freight. The Cork addition strengthens Ireland's access to major U.S. markets, complementing CMA CGM's two-decade presence in Ireland across three ports and up to six weekly sailings, linking the country to more than 420 ports worldwide and supporting key sectors such as pharmaceuticals, agri-food, and manufacturing.

Transportation: There were 6 press releases from 3 private companies in the past week

Sr. No.PermIDCompany NameState Name14295904276CROWLEY MARITIMEFLORIDA24296119761ESTES EXPRESS LINESVIRGINIA3OCEAN NETWORK EXPRESS PTE. LIMITEDSINGAPORE

+ CROWLEY MARITIME [2 Press Releases in Week 18]

DASHBOARD: CROWLEY MARITIME ; FLORIDA

PermID4295904276Websitehttp://www.crowley.com/IndustryTransportationFounded1892

ACTIVITIES:

Crowley Maritime Corporation, founded in 1892, is a family-owned logistics and maritime services company headquartered in Jacksonville, Florida. Employing over 5,300 people, it operates in shipping, energy, and supply chain management, generating over $2 billion annually. Crowley provides ocean transport, LNG distribution, and logistics for government and commercial clients. In 2024, it expanded renewable energy logistics, supporting offshore wind projects. Its culture emphasizes safety, innovation, and sustainability, with robust employee training. Competing with Maersk, Crowley stands out for its U.S.-focused operations and government contracts. Recent initiatives include decarbonization efforts and digital tracking systems, aligning with green logistics trends.

+ Week 18 of 2026 - Press release 1 of 2 Apr 29, 2026:

- CROWLEY MARITIME PEER LANDSCAPE: RECENT NEWS HIGHLIGHTS

+ Week 18 of 2026 - Press release 2 of 2 Apr 30, 2026:

- CROWLEY MARITIME & PEERS: INDUSTRY NEWS DIGEST

+ ESTES EXPRESS LINES [2 Press Releases in Week 18]

DASHBOARD: ESTES EXPRESS LINES; VIRGINIA

PermID4296119761Websitehttps://www.estes-express.com/IndustryTransportationAddress3901 West Broad Street Richmond, Virginia 23230Founded1931

ACTIVITIES:

Estes Express Lines, founded in 1931 by W.W. Estes, is a family-owned freight transportation company headquartered in Richmond, Virginia. With over 22,000 employees and 280 terminals across North America, it generates approximately $4.5 billion in annual revenue. Estes provides less-than-truckload (LTL) shipping, truckload services, and logistics solutions, serving industries like retail and manufacturing. Its proprietary technology enhances tracking and efficiency. In 2023, Estes invested in electric trucks to advance sustainability. Competing with Old Dominion and YRC Freight, its mission is to deliver reliable, innovative freight solutions with a customer-first approach.

+ Week 18 of 2026 - Press release 1 of 2 Apr 28, 2026:

TRACKING TRENDS: ESTES EXPRESS LINES & PEER INSIGHTS

+ Week 18 of 2026 - Press release 2 of 2 Apr 28, 2026:

TRACKING TRENDS: ESTES EXPRESS LINES & PEER INSIGHTS

+ OCEAN NETWORK EXPRESS PTE. LIMITED [2 Press Releases in Week 18]

DASHBOARD: OCEAN NETWORK EXPRESS PTE. LIMITED; SINGAPORE

Websitehttps://www.one-line.com/enIndustryTransportationAddress7 Straits View, #16-01 Marina One East Tower, Singapore 018936Founded2017

ACTIVITIES:

Ocean Network Express (ONE), founded in 2017, is a Singapore-based global container shipping company formed by the merger of Japans NYK, MOL, and K Line. Operating over 200 vessels, ONE handles 1.4 million TEUs annually, ranking among the worlds top shipping lines. It provides container transport services across Asia, Europe, and North America, focusing on reliability, sustainability, and digital innovation. ONEs global network includes 120 ports, supported by 14,000 employees. The company emphasizes eco-friendly practices, investing in low-emission vessels and smart logistics to optimize global trade efficiency.

Accordingly, no warranties or other guarantees are offered as to the quality of the opinions, commentary or anything else appearing in such Newstex Authoritative Content. Newstex and its re-distributors expressly reserve the right to delete stories at its and their sole discretion.

Kyle Jackson

May 11th, 2026 ( Watchlist News - Delivered by Newstex )

Webull ( NASDAQ:BULL - Get Free Report) and Brookfield Asset Management ( NYSE:BAM - Get Free Report) are both finance companies, but which is the superior stock? We will compare the two companies based on the strength of their dividends, valuation, analyst recommendations, profitability, institutional ownership, earnings and risk.

Volatility & Risk

Webull has a beta of 0.6, suggesting that its stock price is 40% less volatile than the S&P 500. Comparatively, Brookfield Asset Management has a beta of 1.28, suggesting that its stock price is 28% more volatile than the S&P 500.

Analyst Ratings

This is a summary of current recommendations for Webull and Brookfield Asset Management, as reported by MarketBeat.com.

Sell RatingsHold RatingsBuy RatingsStrong Buy RatingsRating ScoreWebull11212.60Brookfield Asset Management28712.39

Webull presently has a consensus target price of $13.00, suggesting a potential upside of 83.88%. Brookfield Asset Management has a consensus target price of $60.09, suggesting a potential upside of 22.95%. Given Webull's stronger consensus rating and higher possible upside, analysts plainly believe Webull is more favorable than Brookfield Asset Management.

Earnings and Valuation

This table compares Webull and Brookfield Asset Management's revenue, earnings per share and valuation.

Gross RevenuePrice/Sales RatioNet IncomeEarnings Per SharePrice/Earnings RatioWebull$571.00 million6.58$24.77 million($1.18)-5.99Brookfield Asset Management$4.82 billion16.62$2.49 billion$1.5431.73

Brookfield Asset Management has higher revenue and earnings than Webull. Webull is trading at a lower price-to-earnings ratio than Brookfield Asset Management, indicating that it is currently the more affordable of the two stocks.

Insider & Institutional Ownership

92.5% of Webull shares are owned by institutional investors. Comparatively, 68.4% of Brookfield Asset Management shares are owned by institutional investors. 35.0% of Webull shares are owned by company insiders. Strong institutional ownership is an indication that hedge funds, endowments and large money managers believe a company will outperform the market over the long term.

Profitability

This table compares Webull and Brookfield Asset Management's net margins, return on equity and return on assets.

Net MarginsReturn on EquityReturn on AssetsWebull4.34%34.33%1.47%Brookfield Asset Management49.69%30.26%16.55%

Summary

Brookfield Asset Management beats Webull on 8 of the 13 factors compared between the two stocks.

About Webull

( Get Free Report)

Webull Corp. engages in the provision of financial services. It offers trading, wealth management product distribution, market data and information, user community, and investor education. The company was founded by An Quan Wang in 2016 and is headquartered in St. Petersburg, FL.

About Brookfield Asset Management

( Get Free Report)

Brookfield Asset Management Ltd. is a real estate investment firm specializing in alternative asset management services. Its renewable power and transition business includes the operates in the hydroelectric, wind, solar, distributed generation, and sustainable solution sector. The company's infrastructure business engages in the utilities, transport, midstream, and data infrastructure sectors. In addition, its private equity business offers industrial, infrastructure, and business services sectors; and real estate business, which includes housing, logistics, hospitality, science and innovation, office, and retail sectors. Further, it provides credit business; and insurance solution in the reinsurance, annuities, operating platform, and investment solutions sectors. Brookfield Asset Management Ltd. was incorporated in 2022 and is headquartered in Toronto, Canada.

Fri Apr 24, 2026: Duke Energy Secures 20-Year License for Robinson Nuclear Plant

Duke Energy Corporation has received a significant boost with the U.S. Nuclear Regulatory Commission's (NRC) decision to grant a 20-year extension for the operating license of its Robinson Nuclear Plant. This renewal allows the facility to continue producing energy until the year 2050, a crucial development for the utility giant. The announcement was made on April 23rd, 2026, and signifies Duke Energy's commitment to providing stable and sustainable energy to the region.

The Robinson Nuclear Plant, located in Hartsville, South Carolina, plays a vital role in Duke Energy's operations, supplying electricity to approximately 570,000 households. This license extension not only secures the plant's operational future but also aligns with Duke Energy's broader strategy of maintaining a diverse energy portfolio. The company is focused on integrating nuclear, natural gas, and renewable energy sources to meet the growing energy demands of its 8 million customers across various states, including the Carolinas, Indiana, Florida, Ohio, and Kentucky.

Duke Energy operates within the regulated utilities sector, boasting a market capitalization of approximately $99.64 billion. The company is segmented into electric utilities and infrastructure, gas utilities and infrastructure, and commercial renewables. Currently, the company's P/E ratio stands at 20.26, which is close to its five-year median, indicating that the stock may be fairly valued in the current market context. Investors are encouraged to consider this valuation alongside other financial metrics when evaluating the stock's potential.

The GF Score(TradeMark) for Duke Energy is 77 out of 100, reflecting strong performance across key metrics that can lead to higher long-term returns. The company's profitability rank is notably strong at 7 out of 10, underscoring its operational efficiency. However, concerns regarding financial strength persist, with a rating of just 3 out of 10, suggesting potential issues related to debt levels and overall financial health. Investors should carefully weigh these factors when considering Duke Energy as part of their investment portfolio.

In recent months, insider trading activity has raised eyebrows, with insiders selling $5.6 million worth of shares and no recorded insider buying. This trend may lead to questions about insider confidence in the company's future performance. Overall, Duke Energy presents a mixed outlook for investors. While the recent license renewal for the Robinson Nuclear Plant strengthens its operational foundation, its financial challenges could impact long-term growth. Investors are encouraged to explore further insights about Duke Energy and its current market position.

Recent News 2 of 3, Source: HourlyBusinessNews, 395 words

Wed Apr 22, 2026: Duke Energy Foundation Launches America250 Grants for South Carolina Green Spaces

GREENVILLE, S.C.– To strengthen communities across South Carolina as the nation approaches its 250th anniversary, Duke Energy Foundation is awarding $275,000 to nonprofit organizations through a first round of funding as part of its America250 initiative. This funding, announced on 22nd April 2026, celebrates Earth Day and aims to enhance local parks, tree planting efforts, and trail maintenance projects throughout the Palmetto State.

The grants, which form part of a total investment exceeding $550,000 in South Carolina, are aimed at revitalizing parks and shared green spaces, supporting tree planting, and litter prevention initiatives. "As we look back on our country's 250-year anniversary, it's an opportunity to reflect on our nation's history while working to support the next generation," said Tim Pearson, Duke Energy South Carolina president. "We are proud to partner with local organizations who can help care for the Palmetto State now, so future generations can enjoy its beauty."

Duke Energy Foundation has awarded a total of 11 grants in this first round of funding. Notable recipients include the South Carolina Wildlife Federation, Foothills Trail Conservancy, and the Upstate Greenways & Trails Alliance. Each organization is expected to utilize the funding to enhance local environments and foster community engagement in conservation efforts. For instance, Upstate Greenways & Trails Alliance plans to create a new trail that highlights the scenic beauty of Lake Keowee while educating visitors about the Revolutionary War history in the area.

The positive responses from grant recipients highlight the importance of such initiatives. Matt Flege, Executive Director of Upstate Greenways & Trails Alliance, expressed gratitude for the support, stating, "The America250 grant will allow us to create a new trail showcasing the beauty of Lake Keowee to South Carolina residents and visitors alike." Similarly, Susan H. Wild, Chair of Sumter Green, emphasized how the investment strengthens their commitment to beautifying the community, ensuring that efforts made today will benefit future generations.

These grants mark the first awards under Duke Energy Foundation's America250 initiative, which represents a commitment of over $1 million towards community-driven projects across the company's six-state service territories. Additional grant recipients will be announced later this spring, including initiatives focused on veterans' workforce pathways and enhancing access to history and civics education. Duke Energy Foundation continues to provide nearly $30 million annually in philanthropic support to the communities it serves, reflecting its dedication to environmental stewardship and community development.

Recent News 3 of 3, Source: HourlyBusinessNews, 271 words

Wed Apr 22, 2026: Duke Energy Honored with Arbor Day Foundation’s Tree Line USA Award for 20th Year

Duke Energy Corporation has received national recognition for its tree care practices for the 20th consecutive year. The Arbor Day Foundation named Duke Energy Florida a 'Tree Line USA' utility, citing the company's rigorous tree pruning standards, comprehensive employee training, and public education efforts aimed at reducing power outages and safeguarding electrical infrastructure.

On 22nd April 2026, the Arbor Day Foundation lauded Duke Energy for meeting industry standards for tree care, supporting tree planting initiatives, and engaging in annual arbor education programs. The designation reflects Duke Energy's commitment to sustainable practices, as well as its proactive approach to maintaining the health of urban trees in proximity to electrical lines.

One of Duke Energy's ongoing initiatives includes an annual free tree giveaway for customers through the Arbor Day Foundation's Energy-Saving Trees program. In 2026, the company distributed 1,000 trees to Florida customers, bringing the total number of free trees given away statewide to over 16,000 since 2017. These trees are shipped directly to customers with detailed planting instructions designed to encourage proper placement away from power lines.

Company officials noted that proper tree placement is essential for conserving energy and minimizing the risk of outages during storm and hurricane seasons. Duke Energy also conducts year-round vegetation maintenance near power lines and ramps up these efforts ahead of hurricane season, ensuring a reliable power supply for its customers.

Additionally, Duke Energy reminds customers not to trim trees near power lines themselves and encourages them to contact Sunshine 811 before digging near underground utilities. More information about responsible tree planting near electrical equipment can be found through Duke Energy's Right Tree Right Place program.

value.Title: CROWLEY MARITIME - UPDATES FROM CMA CGM LOGISTICS SA AND PEERS: RECENT INSIGHTS

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JK1-KM23-RS4K-52SP-00000-00CROWLEY MARITIME - UPDATES FROM CMA CGM LOGISTICS SA AND PEERS: RECENT INSIGHTS2026-05-13T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:36:34ZLexisNexisSTOCKCROWLEY MARITIME - UPDATES FROM CMA CGM LOGISTICS SA AND PEERS: RECENT INSIGHTS

DASHBOARD: CROWLEY MARITIME

PermID4295904276Websitehttp://www.crowley.com/IndustryMaritime & Shipping Logistics

ACTIVITIES:

Crowley Maritime Corporation, founded in 1892, is a family-owned logistics and maritime services company headquartered in Jacksonville, Florida. Employing over 5,300 people, it operates in shipping, energy, and supply chain management, generating over $2 billion annually. Crowley provides ocean transport, LNG distribution, and logistics for government and commercial clients. In 2024, it expanded renewable energy logistics, supporting offshore wind projects. Its culture emphasizes safety, innovation, and sustainability, with robust employee training. Competing with Maersk, Crowley stands out for its U.S.-focused operations and government contracts. Recent initiatives include decarbonization efforts and digital tracking systems, aligning with green logistics trends.

+ CROWLEY MARITIME [3 News That Matters]

+ PORT MCKENZIE STUDY SIGNALS CROWLEY MARITIME'S PUSH INTO ALASKA FUEL INFRASTRUCTURE

Crowley Maritime has secured permission to study a potential fuel-terminal development at Port McKenzie, a step in line with its Alaska expansion strategy. The January 7, 2026 announcement outlines a feasibility study for the facility, aimed at capitalizing on rising regional interest.The proposed terminal would help establish a reliable fuel supply chain for marine and land operations in Alaska, supporting local industries amid growing energy demand in the region.Strategically located in the Matanuska-Susitna Borough, the project could streamline Crowley's logistics while attracting other businesses and investors to Port McKenzie, strengthening the borough's economic landscape.Approval of the permit enables Crowley to advance studies and engage with stakeholders, potentially signaling a milestone in its Alaska growth plan and future Port McKenzie investments.

+ CROWLEY MARITIME CONSOLIDATES UNITS TO SCALE SHIPPING, LOGISTICS AND ENERGY SOLUTIONS

Crowley Maritime announced on January 6, 2026 a strategic consolidation creating two divisions: Shipping and Logistics led by James C. Fowler and Energy led by Kollin Fencil, to strengthen execution and accelerate growth across its shipping, logistics and energy arms.The Shipping and Logistics Division merges Crowley's logistics, land transportation and global ship management into a single unit serving commercial and government customers with ocean shipping, inland trucking, multimodal transport and warehousing.The Energy Division brings together Crowley Fuels in Alaska, LNG services and advanced energy solutions to drive innovation and scale in both traditional and emerging markets.Both Fowler and Fencil, who joined Crowley as senior vice presidents in 2023, have overseen improvements in financial performance and operational efficiency. Chief Operating Officer Megan Davidson said the reorganization will enable faster decision-making and greater adaptability to market shifts, helping Crowley maintain its leadership in maritime, energy and logistics.

+ CROWLEY, SEACOR CREATE FAIRWATER HOLDINGS TO EXPAND U.S. PETROLEUM AND CHEMICAL TRANSPORT

Crowley Maritime and SEACOR Holdings have announced Fairwater Holdings, a new joint venture between the two groups to integrate their liquid energy and chemical transport assets and to operate as a Jones Act service provider.The venture will bring together a fleet of 20 ocean-going articulated tug-barges and 11 tankers under long-term charters and will also handle crewing and technical management for 21 third-party vessels.Daniel Thorogood, CEO of Seabulk, will become Fairwater's CEO after closing, with Crowley's leadership signaling a focus on safe, efficient maritime transportation for the U.S. energy sector.Closing conditions and regulatory approvals are required before operations begin, which are expected to start in the first quarter of 2024.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ CMA CGM LOGISTICS [3 News That Matters]

+ MAWANI BROADENS GLOBAL NETWORK AS CMACGM'S OCEAN RISE BEGINS

RIYADH: Mawani has added CMACGM's Ocean Rise service to Jeddah Islamic Port, expanding connectivity to select Asian and European markets. The service began on 3rd of May 2026, linking Jeddah with Kobe, Nagoya, Yokohama; Xiamen, Yantian, Nansha; Rotterdam; Hamburg; and Southampton.The service can handle up to 3,001 TEUs and supports Saudi trade in coordination with international lines as part of the National Transport and Logistics Strategy to position the Kingdom as a three-continent logistics hub.In the period Mawani launched 18 services with total capacity 123,552 TEUs, and port activity grew with March throughput up 6.66% year on year and February 667,882 TEUs.Jeddah Islamic Port remains a key gateway with the facilities to support international trade.

+ CMA CGM BETS ON INDIA AS CABOTAGE CHANGES PUSH FLEET REGISTRATION

CMACGM has transferred two more containerships into the Indian registry, increasing its Indian-flag fleet to six and following earlier moves of four vessels.The Vila do Conde, a 33,434-dwt ship built in 2009 with 2,556 TEU capacity, moved from Malta to India, and CMACGM has also filed papers to transfer the Semarang, built in 2007 with 38,000 DWT and 2,700 TEU.The re-flagging aligns with India's cabotage changes that restrict foreign-flagged vessels from carrying domestic cargo, with January 2026 marking the rollback of a transshipment exemption. CMA CGM has started employing Indian seafarers on its Indian-flag ships, aiming for 1,500 by year-end, and in February 2026 signed a contract with Cochin Shipyard for LNG-powered newbuilds.

+ CMA CGM UNVEILS AI-DRIVEN BLUEPRINT FOR SMARTER, MORE RESILIENT SUPPLY CHAINS IN INDIA

Volatility in supply chains and rising customer expectations are prompting a reinvention of logistics. CMA CGM Group depicts India as both a strategic growth market and a global innovation hub, with AI, ecosystem partnerships, and digital platforms transforming port operations and client experiences. The result is a move from reactive to predictive, intelligent, and increasingly autonomous supply chains.On 28th April 2026, CMA CGM committed to using artificial intelligence to boost operational efficiency in logistics. The focus is on AI-driven tools that optimize supply chain management and reduce disruptions from volatile markets, leveraging analytics and machine learning to build a more resilient network.CMA CGM plans partnerships with Indian tech players to build an ecosystem that supports AI integration in logistics. This collaborative approach speeds innovation and helps position India as a critical global logistics hub. AI is intended to be a core operating element that enables smarter decisions and better service.Additionally, CMACGM is investing in digital platforms that enhance transparency and real-time shipment tracking, empowering businesses to act on insights and improving overall performance. The company aims to create a seamless client experience that supports growth in e-commerce and manufacturing.

value.Title: Solar drone with jumbo jet wingspan broke a flight record-then it crashed

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JJX-Y483-RRY9-B1VS-00000-00Solar drone with jumbo jet wingspan broke a flight record-then it crashed2026-05-13T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:46:31ZLexisNexisMEXICO NEWSSolar drone with jumbo jet wingspan broke a flight record-then it crashedJeremy Hsu

A solar-powered drone has been lost at sea after a record-breaking flight lasting eight days between late April and early May. The crash also marks the untimely demise of the pioneering aircraft Solar Impulse 2, which previously performed the world's first solar-powered crossings of the Atlantic and Pacific oceans before becoming an uncrewed test platform for US military missions.

The carbon-fiber aircraft could perform such feats of aeronautical endurance while running solely on renewable energy and batteries because of a 236-foot (72-meter) wingspan-comparable to a Boeing 747 jumbo jet's wings-covered with more than 17,000 solar cells. The company Skydweller Aero purchased and modified the original Solar Impulse 2 aircraft to become a test platform for "perpetual uncrewed flight" with the capability of carrying up to 800 pounds (363 kilograms) of payload.

Skydweller Aero was conducting test flights for maritime patrol mission scenarios with the US military, and the company also holds contracts with the Navy and Air Force. So the Skydweller drone was operating in that capacity when it took off on its final flight in the early morning hours of April 26.

In the Navy

After departing from Stennis International Airport in Mississippi, the Skydweller drone flew to join the US Navy's annual Fleet Experimentation (FLEX) exercises near Florida's Key West, according to a Skydweller Aero blog post. The Navy's press release describes the FLEX 2026 event as testing AI and drone technologies for maritime patrols "in the fight against transnational organized crime."

As part of the event, the drone used radar along with visual and thermal imaging to observe targets on the water during four days of continuous flight, according to Skydweller Aero. It also acted as a flying communications hub for Navy aircraft and warships while supporting AIS transponder-based tracking of ships in the area.

The Navy also highlighted the demonstration of a "sophisticated kill chain" which incorporated commercial drones with crewed US military helicopters and the Freedom-variant littoral combat ship USS Wichita. Together, such assets "successfully found, fixed, tracked, and targeted a captured drug boat" in a scenario leading up to "kinetic engagements destroying several captured drug boats," according to the Navy's press release.

It is unclear what role the Skydweller drone may have played in the exercise's drug boat scenario. Ars has reached out to the US Navy for comment.

But the naval exercise comes as US Southern Command (SOUTHCOM) has conducted dozens of "lethal kinetic strikes" against alleged drug boats operating in the Caribbean and Pacific since September 2025. The lethal strikes have killed approximately 194 people to date, according to the nonprofit think tank InSight Crime-and legal and human rights experts have said the strikes violate both domestic and international law.

Following the formal end of the Navy exercise on April 30, the Skydweller drone spent several more days demonstrating "extended operational and airspace flexibility within the SOUTHCOM area of responsibility" by flying between Cuba and Mexico's Yucatan Peninsula, according to Skydweller Aero's blog post. The drone eventually positioned itself south of Cuba and north of the Cayman Islands while waiting out a period of bad weather.

Final destination

By the night of May 3, the drone was encountering severe weather conditions that included "extreme vertical air mass variability exceeding 10 times typical climb and descent rates," Skydweller Aero wrote. The company emphasized that all aircraft systems were nominal throughout the flight-but a lack of energy reserves to deal with the extreme weather eventually brought down the drone.

The Skydweller drone was last visible on the flight-tracking service Flight Radar 24 north of Cancun, Mexico, in the early morning hours of May 4. The company described the drone as eventually performing a "controlled water ditching" around 6:30 am Eastern Time, but the aircraft "subsequently sank due to its non-buoyant composite structure."

By the time it went under, the Skydweller drone had performed a record-breaking, solar-powered flight of eight days and 14 minutes-longer than any previous flights as either a drone or crewed aircraft. The company Skydweller Aero commemorated it as an "operational prototype" that had "validated the practical military utility of a persistent, medium-altitude solar aircraft" despite the loss at sea.

Skydweller drone flights in July 2025.

The aircraft's earlier accomplishments will almost certainly endure in the public imagination. Solar Impulse 2 became the first solar-powered aircraft to circle the globe after completing a series of flights between 2015 and 2016. Along the way, it set a world record for the longest flight in a solar-powered plane when André Borschberg piloted the aircraft for 117 hours and 52 minutes-almost five days-during a 5,545-mile (8,924-kilometer) journey between Nagoya, Japan, and Hawaii.

Now, the crash of the Skydweller drone means that the Swiss Museum of Transport in Lucerne won't get to display the historic aircraft per an original agreement with Skydweller Aero, according to SWI Swissinfo. That represents a blow for aviation enthusiasts unless future salvage operations can be carried out.

The pioneering design may nonetheless inspire future solar-powered aircraft for either civilian or military use. Skydweller Aero told Ars that it has no other prototypes immediately ready to replace the lost drone-but the company's blog post described "planned upgrades using existing technology" that could enable future solar-powered drones to better withstand extreme weather conditions. Meanwhile, the Pentagon has proposed investing at least $54 billion into drone warfare systems.

value.Title: CROWLEY MARITIME - CMA CGM LOGISTICS SA AND PEERS: RECENT INDUSTRY UPDATES

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JJT-MCJ3-RRXW-T1DY-00000-00CROWLEY MARITIME - CMA CGM LOGISTICS SA AND PEERS: RECENT INDUSTRY UPDATES2026-05-12T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:36:34ZLexisNexisSTOCKCROWLEY MARITIME - CMA CGM LOGISTICS SA AND PEERS: RECENT INDUSTRY UPDATES

DASHBOARD: CROWLEY MARITIME

PermID4295904276Websitehttp://www.crowley.com/IndustryMaritime & Shipping Logistics

ACTIVITIES:

Crowley Maritime Corporation, founded in 1892, is a family-owned logistics and maritime services company headquartered in Jacksonville, Florida. Employing over 5,300 people, it operates in shipping, energy, and supply chain management, generating over $2 billion annually. Crowley provides ocean transport, LNG distribution, and logistics for government and commercial clients. In 2024, it expanded renewable energy logistics, supporting offshore wind projects. Its culture emphasizes safety, innovation, and sustainability, with robust employee training. Competing with Maersk, Crowley stands out for its U.S.-focused operations and government contracts. Recent initiatives include decarbonization efforts and digital tracking systems, aligning with green logistics trends.

+ CROWLEY MARITIME [3 News That Matters]

+ PORT MCKENZIE STUDY SIGNALS CROWLEY MARITIME'S PUSH INTO ALASKA FUEL INFRASTRUCTURE

Crowley Maritime has secured permission to study a potential fuel-terminal development at Port McKenzie, a step in line with its Alaska expansion strategy. The January 7, 2026 announcement outlines a feasibility study for the facility, aimed at capitalizing on rising regional interest.The proposed terminal would help establish a reliable fuel supply chain for marine and land operations in Alaska, supporting local industries amid growing energy demand in the region.Strategically located in the Matanuska-Susitna Borough, the project could streamline Crowley's logistics while attracting other businesses and investors to Port McKenzie, strengthening the borough's economic landscape.Approval of the permit enables Crowley to advance studies and engage with stakeholders, potentially signaling a milestone in its Alaska growth plan and future Port McKenzie investments.

+ CROWLEY MARITIME CONSOLIDATES UNITS TO SCALE SHIPPING, LOGISTICS AND ENERGY SOLUTIONS

Crowley Maritime announced on January 6, 2026 a strategic consolidation creating two divisions: Shipping and Logistics led by James C. Fowler and Energy led by Kollin Fencil, to strengthen execution and accelerate growth across its shipping, logistics and energy arms.The Shipping and Logistics Division merges Crowley's logistics, land transportation and global ship management into a single unit serving commercial and government customers with ocean shipping, inland trucking, multimodal transport and warehousing.The Energy Division brings together Crowley Fuels in Alaska, LNG services and advanced energy solutions to drive innovation and scale in both traditional and emerging markets.Both Fowler and Fencil, who joined Crowley as senior vice presidents in 2023, have overseen improvements in financial performance and operational efficiency. Chief Operating Officer Megan Davidson said the reorganization will enable faster decision-making and greater adaptability to market shifts, helping Crowley maintain its leadership in maritime, energy and logistics.

+ CROWLEY, SEACOR CREATE FAIRWATER HOLDINGS TO EXPAND U.S. PETROLEUM AND CHEMICAL TRANSPORT

Crowley Maritime and SEACOR Holdings have announced Fairwater Holdings, a new joint venture between the two groups to integrate their liquid energy and chemical transport assets and to operate as a Jones Act service provider.The venture will bring together a fleet of 20 ocean-going articulated tug-barges and 11 tankers under long-term charters and will also handle crewing and technical management for 21 third-party vessels.Daniel Thorogood, CEO of Seabulk, will become Fairwater's CEO after closing, with Crowley's leadership signaling a focus on safe, efficient maritime transportation for the U.S. energy sector.Closing conditions and regulatory approvals are required before operations begin, which are expected to start in the first quarter of 2024.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ CMA CGM LOGISTICS [3 News That Matters]

+ MAWANI BROADENS GLOBAL NETWORK AS CMACGM'S OCEAN RISE BEGINS

RIYADH: Mawani has added CMACGM's Ocean Rise service to Jeddah Islamic Port, expanding connectivity to select Asian and European markets. The service began on 3rd of May 2026, linking Jeddah with Kobe, Nagoya, Yokohama; Xiamen, Yantian, Nansha; Rotterdam; Hamburg; and Southampton.The service can handle up to 3,001 TEUs and supports Saudi trade in coordination with international lines as part of the National Transport and Logistics Strategy to position the Kingdom as a three-continent logistics hub.In the period Mawani launched 18 services with total capacity 123,552 TEUs, and port activity grew with March throughput up 6.66% year on year and February 667,882 TEUs.Jeddah Islamic Port remains a key gateway with the facilities to support international trade.

+ CMA CGM BETS ON INDIA AS CABOTAGE CHANGES PUSH FLEET REGISTRATION

CMACGM has transferred two more containerships into the Indian registry, increasing its Indian-flag fleet to six and following earlier moves of four vessels.The Vila do Conde, a 33,434-dwt ship built in 2009 with 2,556 TEU capacity, moved from Malta to India, and CMACGM has also filed papers to transfer the Semarang, built in 2007 with 38,000 DWT and 2,700 TEU.The re-flagging aligns with India's cabotage changes that restrict foreign-flagged vessels from carrying domestic cargo, with January 2026 marking the rollback of a transshipment exemption. CMA CGM has started employing Indian seafarers on its Indian-flag ships, aiming for 1,500 by year-end, and in February 2026 signed a contract with Cochin Shipyard for LNG-powered newbuilds.

+ CMA CGM UNVEILS AI-DRIVEN BLUEPRINT FOR SMARTER, MORE RESILIENT SUPPLY CHAINS IN INDIA

Volatility in supply chains and rising customer expectations are prompting a reinvention of logistics. CMA CGM Group depicts India as both a strategic growth market and a global innovation hub, with AI, ecosystem partnerships, and digital platforms transforming port operations and client experiences. The result is a move from reactive to predictive, intelligent, and increasingly autonomous supply chains.On 28th April 2026, CMA CGM committed to using artificial intelligence to boost operational efficiency in logistics. The focus is on AI-driven tools that optimize supply chain management and reduce disruptions from volatile markets, leveraging analytics and machine learning to build a more resilient network.CMA CGM plans partnerships with Indian tech players to build an ecosystem that supports AI integration in logistics. This collaborative approach speeds innovation and helps position India as a critical global logistics hub. AI is intended to be a core operating element that enables smarter decisions and better service.Additionally, CMACGM is investing in digital platforms that enhance transparency and real-time shipment tracking, empowering businesses to act on insights and improving overall performance. The company aims to create a seamless client experience that supports growth in e-commerce and manufacturing.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

value.Title: CROWLEY MARITIME - SECTOR NEWS: LEADERS & PEERS UPDATE

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JJK-GMJ3-SF9N-F51Y-00000-00CROWLEY MARITIME - SECTOR NEWS: LEADERS & PEERS UPDATE2026-05-11T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:46:31ZLexisNexisSTOCKCROWLEY MARITIME - SECTOR NEWS: LEADERS & PEERS UPDATE

DASHBOARD: CROWLEY MARITIME

PermID4295904276Websitehttp://www.crowley.com/IndustryMaritime & Shipping Logistics

ACTIVITIES:

Crowley Maritime Corporation, founded in 1892, is a family-owned logistics and maritime services company headquartered in Jacksonville, Florida. Employing over 5,300 people, it operates in shipping, energy, and supply chain management, generating over $2 billion annually. Crowley provides ocean transport, LNG distribution, and logistics for government and commercial clients. In 2024, it expanded renewable energy logistics, supporting offshore wind projects. Its culture emphasizes safety, innovation, and sustainability, with robust employee training. Competing with Maersk, Crowley stands out for its U.S.-focused operations and government contracts. Recent initiatives include decarbonization efforts and digital tracking systems, aligning with green logistics trends.

+ CROWLEY MARITIME [4 Press Releases]

+ CROWLEY MARITIME: AMERICAN ENERGY LNG CARRIER TRANSPORTS MORE THAN 500 MILLION GALLONS TO PUERTO RICO IN FIRST YEAR

Crowley marked one year of operations of American Energy, the first U.S.-flagged liquefied natural gas (LNG) carrier dedicated to serving Puerto Rico. This milestone highlights the company's commitment to reliably providing LNG supply to support the island's energy resilience.

Source: Company Website

+ NEW CROWLEY BUSINESS STRUCTURE ACCELERATES GROWTH WITH FOWLER AND FENCIL AS DIVISION PRESIDENTS

James Fowler is named executive vice president and division president, Shipping and Logistics.

Kollin Fencil is named executive vice president and division president, Energy.

Business lines are consolidated in unified divisions for streamlined operations and customer value.

Crowley announced today a strategic consolidation of its business structure to create more efficiency and value for customers. The company is creating two divisions: shipping and logistics, led by Executive Vice President and Division President James C. Fowler, and energy, led by Executive Vice President and Division President Kollin Fencil.

Source: Company Website

+ CROWLEY EARNS ENVIRONMENTAL ACHIEVEMENT HONORS COMBINED 958 YEARS OF SAFE OPERATIONS

Crowley has been recognized by the Chamber of Shipping of America (CSA) for its commitment to environmental stewardship, earning Environmental Achievement Awards for 108 Crowley-operated vessels that have collectively earned 958 years without an incident.

The awards celebrate companies and mariners who maintain the highest standards of safety and ecological protection. Crowley's achievement reflects its dedication to operational excellence across a diverse owned and managed fleet around the world.

Source: Company Website

+ CROWLEY EARNS ENVIRONMENTAL ACHIEVEMENT HONORS FOR COMBINED 958 YEARS OF SAFE OPERATIONS

Crowley has been recognized by the Chamber of Shipping of America (CSA) for its commitment to environmental stewardship, earning Environmental Achievement Awards for 108 Crowley-operated vessels that have collectively earned 958 years without an incident.

The awards celebrate companies and mariners who maintain the highest standards of safety and ecological protection. Crowley's achievement reflects its dedication to operational excellence across a diverse owned and managed fleet around the world.

"This recognition by the Chamber of Shipping of America is a powerful reflection of Crowley's unwavering focus on both operational safety and responsible stewardship," said Tucker Gilliam, vice president of fleet operations for Crowley Shipping. "This achievement celebrates our world-class mariners and shoreside operations teams, whose vigilance, professionalism and daily drive to uphold the highest standards are truly what deliver on this promise."

Source: Company Website

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ CMA CGM LOGISTICS SA [4 Press Releases]

+ CMA CGM LOGISTICS SA: FAK RATES - FROM ASIA TO NORTH EUROPE

Please find the new applicable CMA CGM Freight All Kinds (FAK) rates (*) as follows as from May 15th, 2026 (loading date) until further notice:

20'GP 40'GP/40'HC/40'REEFER Asia to North Europe USD 1,950 USD 3,500

Base port to base port rates. Out-ports are subject to usual TAO/TAD.

These new FAK rates will apply as follows:

- Origin Range: From all Asian ports (including Japan, South East Asia & Bangladesh) - Destination Range: To all North European ports (including UK and the full range from Portugal to Finland/Estonia) - Cargo: Dry, OOG, Paying empties & Reefer - Date of application: From May 15th, 2026 (date of loading in the origin ports) until further notice

Source: Company Website

+ CMA CGM LOGISTICS SA: PSS - FROM EAST MEDITERRANEAN TO USA

In a continued effort to provide our customers with reliable and efficient services, CMA CGM Group wishes to inform of the following Peak Season Surcharge (PSS)* : From May 15th, 2026 until further notice:

- Origin range: From East Mediterranean including Turkiye, Greece, Lebanon, Bulgaria, Egypt, Syria, Georgia, Ukraine, Romania, Croatia, Albania, Slovenia

- Destination range: To the US East Coast, US West Coast, US Gulf Coast and all inland destinations reached via said ports

- Cargo: all except OOG

- Amounts: USD 1,050 per 20' | USD 2,100 per 40'/40'HC/45'

Source: Company Website

+ CMA CGM LOGISTICS SA: FAK RATES - FROM ASIA TO THE MEDITERRANEAN & NORTH AFRICA

In a continued effort to provide our customers with reliable and efficient service, CMA CGM announces an increase on our Freight All Kind (FAK) rates as follows as from May 15th to 31st, 2026.

20' 40'/40' HC WEST MED USD 3,300 USD 4,600 ADRIATIC USD 3,400 USD 4,700 EAST MED USD 3,500 USD 4,800 BLACK SEA USD 3,500 USD 4,800 ALGERIA USD 5,100 USD 7,200

These new FAK rates will be applied as follows:

- Origin Range: From all Asian main ports

- Destination range: To all Mediterranean ports (areas mentioned above)

- Cargo: Dry, Reefer, OOG, Paying Empties

- Date of application: May 15th to 31st, 2026 Source: Company Website

+ CMA CGM LOGISTICS SA: BAF UPDATE - TUNISIA RORO SERVICE

Following bunker prices evolution since the last surcharge adjustment, CMA CGM SSL Intra Europe will adjust BAF as follows on its Roro South service connecting France with Tunisia (import & export): Effective May 1st, 2026: Cargo RORO Tunisia service Roll, full EUR 649/unit Roll, empty EUR 430/unit Other rolling units* EUR 73/LM Pass. cars, minivans EUR 248/unit Breakbulk EUR 41/CBM

(*) Truck/Earth moving equipment, on wheels or tracks, duty trucks and agricultural devices, livestock trucks

For any questions or additional information, please contact your local CMA CGM office.

value.Title: CROWLEY MARITIME - RECENT NEWS OF PEER AND INDUSTRY INSIGHTS

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JJK-N943-S0TN-F3S4-00000-00CROWLEY MARITIME - RECENT NEWS OF PEER AND INDUSTRY INSIGHTS2026-05-11T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:36:50ZLexisNexisSTOCKCROWLEY MARITIME - RECENT NEWS OF PEER AND INDUSTRY INSIGHTS

DASHBOARD: CROWLEY MARITIME

PermID4295904276Websitehttp://www.crowley.com/IndustryMaritime & Shipping Logistics

ACTIVITIES:

Crowley Maritime Corporation, founded in 1892, is a family-owned logistics and maritime services company headquartered in Jacksonville, Florida. Employing over 5,300 people, it operates in shipping, energy, and supply chain management, generating over $2 billion annually. Crowley provides ocean transport, LNG distribution, and logistics for government and commercial clients. In 2024, it expanded renewable energy logistics, supporting offshore wind projects. Its culture emphasizes safety, innovation, and sustainability, with robust employee training. Competing with Maersk, Crowley stands out for its U.S.-focused operations and government contracts. Recent initiatives include decarbonization efforts and digital tracking systems, aligning with green logistics trends.

+ CROWLEY MARITIME [3 News That Matters]

+ PORT MCKENZIE STUDY SIGNALS CROWLEY MARITIME'S PUSH INTO ALASKA FUEL INFRASTRUCTURE

Crowley Maritime has secured permission to study a potential fuel-terminal development at Port McKenzie, a step in line with its Alaska expansion strategy. The January 7, 2026 announcement outlines a feasibility study for the facility, aimed at capitalizing on rising regional interest.The proposed terminal would help establish a reliable fuel supply chain for marine and land operations in Alaska, supporting local industries amid growing energy demand in the region.Strategically located in the Matanuska-Susitna Borough, the project could streamline Crowley's logistics while attracting other businesses and investors to Port McKenzie, strengthening the borough's economic landscape.Approval of the permit enables Crowley to advance studies and engage with stakeholders, potentially signaling a milestone in its Alaska growth plan and future Port McKenzie investments.

+ CROWLEY MARITIME CONSOLIDATES UNITS TO SCALE SHIPPING, LOGISTICS AND ENERGY SOLUTIONS

Crowley Maritime announced on January 6, 2026 a strategic consolidation creating two divisions: Shipping and Logistics led by James C. Fowler and Energy led by Kollin Fencil, to strengthen execution and accelerate growth across its shipping, logistics and energy arms.The Shipping and Logistics Division merges Crowley's logistics, land transportation and global ship management into a single unit serving commercial and government customers with ocean shipping, inland trucking, multimodal transport and warehousing.The Energy Division brings together Crowley Fuels in Alaska, LNG services and advanced energy solutions to drive innovation and scale in both traditional and emerging markets.Both Fowler and Fencil, who joined Crowley as senior vice presidents in 2023, have overseen improvements in financial performance and operational efficiency. Chief Operating Officer Megan Davidson said the reorganization will enable faster decision-making and greater adaptability to market shifts, helping Crowley maintain its leadership in maritime, energy and logistics.

+ CROWLEY, SEACOR CREATE FAIRWATER HOLDINGS TO EXPAND U.S. PETROLEUM AND CHEMICAL TRANSPORT

Crowley Maritime and SEACOR Holdings have announced Fairwater Holdings, a new joint venture between the two groups to integrate their liquid energy and chemical transport assets and to operate as a Jones Act service provider.The venture will bring together a fleet of 20 ocean-going articulated tug-barges and 11 tankers under long-term charters and will also handle crewing and technical management for 21 third-party vessels.Daniel Thorogood, CEO of Seabulk, will become Fairwater's CEO after closing, with Crowley's leadership signaling a focus on safe, efficient maritime transportation for the U.S. energy sector.Closing conditions and regulatory approvals are required before operations begin, which are expected to start in the first quarter of 2024.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ CMA CGM LOGISTICS [3 News That Matters]

+ MAWANI BROADENS GLOBAL NETWORK AS CMACGM'S OCEAN RISE BEGINS

RIYADH: Mawani has added CMACGM's Ocean Rise service to Jeddah Islamic Port, expanding connectivity to select Asian and European markets. The service began on 3rd of May 2026, linking Jeddah with Kobe, Nagoya, Yokohama; Xiamen, Yantian, Nansha; Rotterdam; Hamburg; and Southampton.The service can handle up to 3,001 TEUs and supports Saudi trade in coordination with international lines as part of the National Transport and Logistics Strategy to position the Kingdom as a three-continent logistics hub.In the period Mawani launched 18 services with total capacity 123,552 TEUs, and port activity grew with March throughput up 6.66% year on year and February 667,882 TEUs.Jeddah Islamic Port remains a key gateway with the facilities to support international trade.

+ CMA CGM BETS ON INDIA AS CABOTAGE CHANGES PUSH FLEET REGISTRATION

CMACGM has transferred two more containerships into the Indian registry, increasing its Indian-flag fleet to six and following earlier moves of four vessels.The Vila do Conde, a 33,434-dwt ship built in 2009 with 2,556 TEU capacity, moved from Malta to India, and CMACGM has also filed papers to transfer the Semarang, built in 2007 with 38,000 DWT and 2,700 TEU.The re-flagging aligns with India's cabotage changes that restrict foreign-flagged vessels from carrying domestic cargo, with January 2026 marking the rollback of a transshipment exemption. CMA CGM has started employing Indian seafarers on its Indian-flag ships, aiming for 1,500 by year-end, and in February 2026 signed a contract with Cochin Shipyard for LNG-powered newbuilds.

+ CMA CGM UNVEILS AI-DRIVEN BLUEPRINT FOR SMARTER, MORE RESILIENT SUPPLY CHAINS IN INDIA

Volatility in supply chains and rising customer expectations are prompting a reinvention of logistics. CMA CGM Group depicts India as both a strategic growth market and a global innovation hub, with AI, ecosystem partnerships, and digital platforms transforming port operations and client experiences. The result is a move from reactive to predictive, intelligent, and increasingly autonomous supply chains.On 28th April 2026, CMA CGM committed to using artificial intelligence to boost operational efficiency in logistics. The focus is on AI-driven tools that optimize supply chain management and reduce disruptions from volatile markets, leveraging analytics and machine learning to build a more resilient network.CMA CGM plans partnerships with Indian tech players to build an ecosystem that supports AI integration in logistics. This collaborative approach speeds innovation and helps position India as a critical global logistics hub. AI is intended to be a core operating element that enables smarter decisions and better service.Additionally, CMACGM is investing in digital platforms that enhance transparency and real-time shipment tracking, empowering businesses to act on insights and improving overall performance. The company aims to create a seamless client experience that supports growth in e-commerce and manufacturing.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

value.Title: CROWLEY MARITIME - SECTOR HIGHLIGHTS INCLUDING PEERS

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JJF-9X33-S406-143B-00000-00CROWLEY MARITIME - SECTOR HIGHLIGHTS INCLUDING PEERS2026-05-11T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:46:31ZLexisNexisSTOCKCROWLEY MARITIME - SECTOR HIGHLIGHTS INCLUDING PEERS

DASHBOARD: CROWLEY MARITIME

PermID4295904276Websitehttp://www.crowley.com/IndustryMaritime & Shipping Logistics

ACTIVITIES:

Crowley Maritime Corporation, founded in 1892, is a family-owned logistics and maritime services company headquartered in Jacksonville, Florida. Employing over 5,300 people, it operates in shipping, energy, and supply chain management, generating over $2 billion annually. Crowley provides ocean transport, LNG distribution, and logistics for government and commercial clients. In 2024, it expanded renewable energy logistics, supporting offshore wind projects. Its culture emphasizes safety, innovation, and sustainability, with robust employee training. Competing with Maersk, Crowley stands out for its U.S.-focused operations and government contracts. Recent initiatives include decarbonization efforts and digital tracking systems, aligning with green logistics trends.

+ CROWLEY MARITIME [4 Press Releases]

+ CROWLEY MARITIME: AMERICAN ENERGY LNG CARRIER TRANSPORTS MORE THAN 500 MILLION GALLONS TO PUERTO RICO IN FIRST YEAR

Crowley marked one year of operations of American Energy, the first U.S.-flagged liquefied natural gas (LNG) carrier dedicated to serving Puerto Rico. This milestone highlights the company's commitment to reliably providing LNG supply to support the island's energy resilience.

Source: Company Website

+ NEW CROWLEY BUSINESS STRUCTURE ACCELERATES GROWTH WITH FOWLER AND FENCIL AS DIVISION PRESIDENTS

James Fowler is named executive vice president and division president, Shipping and Logistics.

Kollin Fencil is named executive vice president and division president, Energy.

Business lines are consolidated in unified divisions for streamlined operations and customer value.

Crowley announced today a strategic consolidation of its business structure to create more efficiency and value for customers. The company is creating two divisions: shipping and logistics, led by Executive Vice President and Division President James C. Fowler, and energy, led by Executive Vice President and Division President Kollin Fencil.

Source: Company Website

+ CROWLEY EARNS ENVIRONMENTAL ACHIEVEMENT HONORS COMBINED 958 YEARS OF SAFE OPERATIONS

Crowley has been recognized by the Chamber of Shipping of America (CSA) for its commitment to environmental stewardship, earning Environmental Achievement Awards for 108 Crowley-operated vessels that have collectively earned 958 years without an incident.

The awards celebrate companies and mariners who maintain the highest standards of safety and ecological protection. Crowley's achievement reflects its dedication to operational excellence across a diverse owned and managed fleet around the world.

Source: Company Website

+ CROWLEY EARNS ENVIRONMENTAL ACHIEVEMENT HONORS FOR COMBINED 958 YEARS OF SAFE OPERATIONS

Crowley has been recognized by the Chamber of Shipping of America (CSA) for its commitment to environmental stewardship, earning Environmental Achievement Awards for 108 Crowley-operated vessels that have collectively earned 958 years without an incident.

The awards celebrate companies and mariners who maintain the highest standards of safety and ecological protection. Crowley's achievement reflects its dedication to operational excellence across a diverse owned and managed fleet around the world.

"This recognition by the Chamber of Shipping of America is a powerful reflection of Crowley's unwavering focus on both operational safety and responsible stewardship," said Tucker Gilliam, vice president of fleet operations for Crowley Shipping. "This achievement celebrates our world-class mariners and shoreside operations teams, whose vigilance, professionalism and daily drive to uphold the highest standards are truly what deliver on this promise."

Source: Company Website

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ CMA CGM LOGISTICS SA [4 Press Releases]

+ CMA CGM LOGISTICS SA: FAK RATES - FROM ASIA TO NORTH EUROPE

Please find the new applicable CMA CGM Freight All Kinds (FAK) rates (*) as follows as from May 15th, 2026 (loading date) until further notice:

20'GP 40'GP/40'HC/40'REEFER Asia to North Europe USD 1,950 USD 3,500

Base port to base port rates. Out-ports are subject to usual TAO/TAD.

These new FAK rates will apply as follows:

- Origin Range: From all Asian ports (including Japan, South East Asia & Bangladesh) - Destination Range: To all North European ports (including UK and the full range from Portugal to Finland/Estonia) - Cargo: Dry, OOG, Paying empties & Reefer - Date of application: From May 15th, 2026 (date of loading in the origin ports) until further notice

Source: Company Website

+ CMA CGM LOGISTICS SA: PSS - FROM EAST MEDITERRANEAN TO USA

In a continued effort to provide our customers with reliable and efficient services, CMA CGM Group wishes to inform of the following Peak Season Surcharge (PSS)* : From May 15th, 2026 until further notice:

- Origin range: From East Mediterranean including Turkiye, Greece, Lebanon, Bulgaria, Egypt, Syria, Georgia, Ukraine, Romania, Croatia, Albania, Slovenia

- Destination range: To the US East Coast, US West Coast, US Gulf Coast and all inland destinations reached via said ports

- Cargo: all except OOG

- Amounts: USD 1,050 per 20' | USD 2,100 per 40'/40'HC/45'

Source: Company Website

+ CMA CGM LOGISTICS SA: FAK RATES - FROM ASIA TO THE MEDITERRANEAN & NORTH AFRICA

In a continued effort to provide our customers with reliable and efficient service, CMA CGM announces an increase on our Freight All Kind (FAK) rates as follows as from May 15th to 31st, 2026.

20' 40'/40' HC WEST MED USD 3,300 USD 4,600 ADRIATIC USD 3,400 USD 4,700 EAST MED USD 3,500 USD 4,800 BLACK SEA USD 3,500 USD 4,800 ALGERIA USD 5,100 USD 7,200

These new FAK rates will be applied as follows:

- Origin Range: From all Asian main ports

- Destination range: To all Mediterranean ports (areas mentioned above)

- Cargo: Dry, Reefer, OOG, Paying Empties

- Date of application: May 15th to 31st, 2026 Source: Company Website

+ CMA CGM LOGISTICS SA: BAF UPDATE - TUNISIA RORO SERVICE

Following bunker prices evolution since the last surcharge adjustment, CMA CGM SSL Intra Europe will adjust BAF as follows on its Roro South service connecting France with Tunisia (import & export): Effective May 1st, 2026: Cargo RORO Tunisia service Roll, full EUR 649/unit Roll, empty EUR 430/unit Other rolling units* EUR 73/LM Pass. cars, minivans EUR 248/unit Breakbulk EUR 41/CBM

(*) Truck/Earth moving equipment, on wheels or tracks, duty trucks and agricultural devices, livestock trucks

For any questions or additional information, please contact your local CMA CGM office.

Source: Company Website

value.Title: CROWLEY MARITIME - THE RECENT SECTOR NEWS

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JJG-4V63-RY9N-X022-00000-00CROWLEY MARITIME - THE RECENT SECTOR NEWS2026-05-11T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:46:31ZLexisNexisSTOCKCROWLEY MARITIME - THE RECENT SECTOR NEWS

DASHBOARD: CROWLEY MARITIME

PermID4295904276Websitehttp://www.crowley.com/IndustryMaritime & Shipping Logistics

ACTIVITIES:

Crowley Maritime Corporation, founded in 1892, is a family-owned logistics and maritime services company headquartered in Jacksonville, Florida. Employing over 5,300 people, it operates in shipping, energy, and supply chain management, generating over $2 billion annually. Crowley provides ocean transport, LNG distribution, and logistics for government and commercial clients. In 2024, it expanded renewable energy logistics, supporting offshore wind projects. Its culture emphasizes safety, innovation, and sustainability, with robust employee training. Competing with Maersk, Crowley stands out for its U.S.-focused operations and government contracts. Recent initiatives include decarbonization efforts and digital tracking systems, aligning with green logistics trends.

+ CROWLEY MARITIME [4 Press Releases]

+ CROWLEY MARITIME: AMERICAN ENERGY LNG CARRIER TRANSPORTS MORE THAN 500 MILLION GALLONS TO PUERTO RICO IN FIRST YEAR

Crowley marked one year of operations of American Energy, the first U.S.-flagged liquefied natural gas (LNG) carrier dedicated to serving Puerto Rico. This milestone highlights the company's commitment to reliably providing LNG supply to support the island's energy resilience.

Source: Company Website

+ NEW CROWLEY BUSINESS STRUCTURE ACCELERATES GROWTH WITH FOWLER AND FENCIL AS DIVISION PRESIDENTS

James Fowler is named executive vice president and division president, Shipping and Logistics.

Kollin Fencil is named executive vice president and division president, Energy.

Business lines are consolidated in unified divisions for streamlined operations and customer value.

Crowley announced today a strategic consolidation of its business structure to create more efficiency and value for customers. The company is creating two divisions: shipping and logistics, led by Executive Vice President and Division President James C. Fowler, and energy, led by Executive Vice President and Division President Kollin Fencil.

Source: Company Website

+ CROWLEY EARNS ENVIRONMENTAL ACHIEVEMENT HONORS COMBINED 958 YEARS OF SAFE OPERATIONS

Crowley has been recognized by the Chamber of Shipping of America (CSA) for its commitment to environmental stewardship, earning Environmental Achievement Awards for 108 Crowley-operated vessels that have collectively earned 958 years without an incident.

The awards celebrate companies and mariners who maintain the highest standards of safety and ecological protection. Crowley's achievement reflects its dedication to operational excellence across a diverse owned and managed fleet around the world.

Source: Company Website

+ CROWLEY EARNS ENVIRONMENTAL ACHIEVEMENT HONORS FOR COMBINED 958 YEARS OF SAFE OPERATIONS

Crowley has been recognized by the Chamber of Shipping of America (CSA) for its commitment to environmental stewardship, earning Environmental Achievement Awards for 108 Crowley-operated vessels that have collectively earned 958 years without an incident.

The awards celebrate companies and mariners who maintain the highest standards of safety and ecological protection. Crowley's achievement reflects its dedication to operational excellence across a diverse owned and managed fleet around the world.

"This recognition by the Chamber of Shipping of America is a powerful reflection of Crowley's unwavering focus on both operational safety and responsible stewardship," said Tucker Gilliam, vice president of fleet operations for Crowley Shipping. "This achievement celebrates our world-class mariners and shoreside operations teams, whose vigilance, professionalism and daily drive to uphold the highest standards are truly what deliver on this promise."

Source: Company Website

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ CMA CGM LOGISTICS SA [4 Press Releases]

+ CMA CGM LOGISTICS SA: FAK RATES - FROM ASIA TO NORTH EUROPE

Please find the new applicable CMA CGM Freight All Kinds (FAK) rates (*) as follows as from May 15th, 2026 (loading date) until further notice:

20'GP 40'GP/40'HC/40'REEFER Asia to North Europe USD 1,950 USD 3,500

Base port to base port rates. Out-ports are subject to usual TAO/TAD.

These new FAK rates will apply as follows:

- Origin Range: From all Asian ports (including Japan, South East Asia & Bangladesh) - Destination Range: To all North European ports (including UK and the full range from Portugal to Finland/Estonia) - Cargo: Dry, OOG, Paying empties & Reefer - Date of application: From May 15th, 2026 (date of loading in the origin ports) until further notice

Source: Company Website

+ CMA CGM LOGISTICS SA: PSS - FROM EAST MEDITERRANEAN TO USA

In a continued effort to provide our customers with reliable and efficient services, CMA CGM Group wishes to inform of the following Peak Season Surcharge (PSS)* : From May 15th, 2026 until further notice:

- Origin range: From East Mediterranean including Turkiye, Greece, Lebanon, Bulgaria, Egypt, Syria, Georgia, Ukraine, Romania, Croatia, Albania, Slovenia

- Destination range: To the US East Coast, US West Coast, US Gulf Coast and all inland destinations reached via said ports

- Cargo: all except OOG

- Amounts: USD 1,050 per 20' | USD 2,100 per 40'/40'HC/45'

Source: Company Website

+ CMA CGM LOGISTICS SA: FAK RATES - FROM ASIA TO THE MEDITERRANEAN & NORTH AFRICA

In a continued effort to provide our customers with reliable and efficient service, CMA CGM announces an increase on our Freight All Kind (FAK) rates as follows as from May 15th to 31st, 2026.

20' 40'/40' HC WEST MED USD 3,300 USD 4,600 ADRIATIC USD 3,400 USD 4,700 EAST MED USD 3,500 USD 4,800 BLACK SEA USD 3,500 USD 4,800 ALGERIA USD 5,100 USD 7,200

These new FAK rates will be applied as follows:

- Origin Range: From all Asian main ports

- Destination range: To all Mediterranean ports (areas mentioned above)

- Cargo: Dry, Reefer, OOG, Paying Empties

- Date of application: May 15th to 31st, 2026 Source: Company Website

+ CMA CGM LOGISTICS SA: BAF UPDATE - TUNISIA RORO SERVICE

Following bunker prices evolution since the last surcharge adjustment, CMA CGM SSL Intra Europe will adjust BAF as follows on its Roro South service connecting France with Tunisia (import & export): Effective May 1st, 2026: Cargo RORO Tunisia service Roll, full EUR 649/unit Roll, empty EUR 430/unit Other rolling units* EUR 73/LM Pass. cars, minivans EUR 248/unit Breakbulk EUR 41/CBM

(*) Truck/Earth moving equipment, on wheels or tracks, duty trucks and agricultural devices, livestock trucks

For any questions or additional information, please contact your local CMA CGM office.

Source: Company Website

value.Title: Constellation Energy May Evaluate Positioning Amid NRG Energy Trademark Challenge In NextNRG Dispute *Updated*

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JJG-08W3-RYWB-C07D-00000-00Constellation Energy May Evaluate Positioning Amid NRG Energy Trademark Challenge In NextNRG Dispute *Updated*2026-05-11T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:46:31ZLexisNexisConstellation Energy May Evaluate Positioning Amid NRG Energy Trademark Challenge In NextNRG Dispute *Updated*

NYSE STOCK REPORT

What Stakeholders Need To Know:

This matter highlights risks to established energy brands from emerging participants adopting similar nomenclature in renewables and EV infrastructure. The outcomes could influence how U.S. courts evaluate consumer confusion in sector-specific naming, raising compliance costs for brand monitoring across power generation and clean energy transitions. Broader industry effects include increased vigilance on intellectual property in a consolidating market, where clear differentiation supports customer retention and investor confidence amid regulatory shifts toward sustainable solutions. Effective management may limit dilution while reinforcing market positioning.

Background of the Case

NRG Energy, Inc. initiated legal proceedings on April 18, 2025 (Case No. 4:2025cv01790, S.D. Tex., Judge Lee H. Rosenthal) against NextNRG Holding Corp et al. (including NextNRG LLC, NextCharging LLC, and related Miami-based entities formerly tied to EzFill Holdings, Inc., rebranded in February 2025). The complaint alleges trademark infringement under 15 U.S.C. § 1114 (Lanham Act), claiming the defendants' use of "NEXT NRG" and related marks in EV charging, microgrids, AI-driven renewables, and energy services creates a likelihood of confusion with NRG's established "NRG" family of trademarks. NRG requests a permanent injunction, damages, profits, and attorney fees. The case remains in early pre-trial stages with a jury demand.

Competitive Impacts on Constellation Energy

Constellation Energy, a participant in nuclear and renewables, maintains distinct branding that aids its positioning in competitive power markets. The dispute may prompt broader sector reviews of naming strategies in emerging technologies like AI-integrated energy solutions and EV infrastructure. This could indirectly support organizations with well-differentiated identities by reducing potential market confusion. Industry sentiment indicates an increased focus on intellectual property protection, which may stabilize customer acquisition in retail and wholesale segments.

Possible Opportunities for Constellation Energy

Greater emphasis on proprietary clean energy platforms with protected naming.

Strengthened B2B relationships leveraging established brand equity.

Potential for joint ventures featuring clearer intellectual property boundaries.

Enhanced visibility in sustainability and governance communications.

Why This Case Is Challenging for Constellation Energy

Indirect spillover from elevated trademark scrutiny in the energy sector could increase compliance costs for name clearances, portfolio audits, and monitoring, particularly in innovation-driven areas such as advanced nuclear and renewable integrations. This adds layers of complexity to strategic planning amid ongoing regulatory evolution and competition in the clean energy transition. Precedent from the case may influence how similarity is assessed across sub-sectors, prompting additional investor and analyst inquiries on brand risks.

Key Challenges for Constellation Energy

Sector-wide increase in intellectual property diligence expenses for new technology initiatives.

Potential customer or partner inquiries regarding brand distinctions.

Requirement for proactive audits of expanding service and product lines.

Competitive dynamics requiring refined differentiation in marketing.

Constellation Energy's Strategic Responses

Integrate intellectual property assessments into innovation and acquisition pipelines.

Develop and enforce internal branding guidelines.

Collaborate with industry associations on established practices.

Closely monitor developments in the NRG matter.

Key Measures:

These responses can be rolled out through cross-functional teams combining legal, marketing, and strategy functions, ensuring alignment and minimal operational disruption while building resilience.

Possible Losses and Unexpected Advantages

Potential Advantages

Market share increase in clearly differentiated clean energy segments.

Revenue support from enhanced customer trust and retention.

Improved investor perception of governance and risk management.

Smoother advancement of innovation projects without naming conflicts.

Potential Losses

Incremental compliance and legal review costs.

Temporary resource diversion for marketing and audit adjustments.

Extended timelines for new initiative launches.

Broader sector legal and diligence expenses impacting margins.

Final Thoughts

Constellation Energy can utilize sector-wide attention to branding to reinforce its distinct identity and governance standards. A proactive approach to intellectual property management, balanced with growth initiatives in clean power, supports long-term positioning in the evolving energy landscape while mitigating indirect challenges from heightened scrutiny.
